date_sunset_snippet: Sunset provision: section repealed January 1, 2029 (per § 3212.15(g)). | disability retirement presumption (county pension systems) — repealed January 1, 2032 per SB 623 extensio
notes: Sunset provision: section repealed January 1, 2029 (per § 3212.15(g)). Applies to injuries occurring on or after January 1, 2020. Covers same firefighter population as § 3212.1 plus peace officers (§ 830.1, §830.2(a)(b)(c), §830.32, §830.37(a)(b), §830.5, §830.55) and Cal OES fire/rescue coordinators. Government Code § 31720.91 provides parallel disability retirement presumption (county pension systems) — repealed January 1, 2032 per SB 623 extension.

Tobacco exclusion: does not apply to firefighters with history of tobacco use for cardiovascular or lung conditions. Annual medical exam required for each of first 7 years. Firefighter must hold at minimum Fire Fighter I certification. Presumption may be rebutted by preponderance including tobacco use, fitness, lifestyle, hereditary factors, and other exposures. Covers firefighters under AS 23.30.243 (WC-covered fire fighters).

Event must occur within 72 hours after exposure to smoke, fumes, or toxic substances. Tobacco exclusion applies. Annual medical exam required for each of first 7 years. Fire Fighter I certification required. Presumption rebutted by preponderance.

Firefighter must demonstrate exposure to a known IARC or NTP carcinogen associated with the disabling cancer. Annual medical exam required for each of first 7 years. Fire Fighter I certification required. Presumption covers paid and registered volunteer firefighters.
'Fire fighter' defined to include members of volunteer fire departments registered with state fire marshal or registered for WC purposes. Same terms and conditions as career firefighters.
Volunteer firefighters registered with state fire marshal or for WC. Same terms as career firefighters.
Volunteer firefighters registered with state fire marshal or for WC. Same terms as career firefighters. Carcinogen exposure demonstration required.

date_effective_snippet: ore Sept 8, 1967 (municipal) or before Jan 1, 2013 (state). Effective October 1, 2026.
notes: Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence. Applies to city/municipal firefighters (§11-43-144) and state-employed firefighters (§36-30-40/41). Both sections use identical occupational disease list. Pre-employment exam required unless hired before Sept 8, 1967 (municipal) or before Jan 1, 2013 (state). Effective October 1, 2026.
Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence. Applies to city/municipal firefighters (§11-43-144) and state-employed firefighters (§36-30-40/41). Effective October 1, 2026.

date_effective_snippet: rmination window of 10 years applies to death benefit only. Effective October 1, 2026.
notes: Must manifest during service AND firefighter must demonstrate exposure to known IARC carcinogen. Rebuttable by preponderance. Death from cancer within 10 years of last employment is treated as line-of-duty death (§11-43-144(c)(2)). Post-termination window of 10 years applies to death benefit only. Effective October 1, 2026.

date_effective_snippet: strate line-of-scope exposure. Rebuttable by preponderance. Effective October 1, 2026.
notes: Must manifest during employment AND firefighter must demonstrate line-of-scope exposure. Rebuttable by preponderance. Effective October 1, 2026.

date_effective_snippet: strate line-of-scope exposure. Rebuttable by preponderance. Effective October 1, 2026.
notes: Must manifest during service AND firefighter must demonstrate line-of-scope exposure. Rebuttable by preponderance. Effective October 1, 2026.

date_effective_snippet: ociated with Parkinson's risk. Rebuttable by preponderance. Effective October 1, 2026.
notes: Must manifest during service AND firefighter must demonstrate exposure to known toxin associated with Parkinson's risk. Rebuttable by preponderance. Effective October 1, 2026.

Three statutory mechanisms: (1) § 21-5-705 provides death benefit up to $150,000 for firefighters killed in line of duty from covered cancers after Jan 1, 2012 — firefighter must be under 68; Firefighter Benefit Review Panel makes recommendations to Arkansas State Claims Commission. (2) § 24-4-511 (APERS) and § 24-10-607 (LOPFI) provide disability retirement presumption for cancer diagnosed 'while in the line of duty.' Both rebuttable by preponderance. § 24-4-511 requires 5 years actual service including 18 of last 24 months; members hired after July 1, 2013 under § 24-10-607 require 10 years. Firefighter must not have been diagnosed with cancer prior to service start. LOPFI section also covers volunteer members.

Both APERS (§ 24-4-511) and LOPFI (§ 24-10-607) explicitly extend cancer presumption to 'volunteer firefighter' / 'volunteer member.' Rebuttable by preponderance. Cancer must be diagnosed 'while in the line of duty.'

Applies to firefighters and peace officers with identical terms for this cancer list. Must be assigned to hazardous duty for at least 5 years. Must have been exposed to known IARC carcinogen and informed department of exposure. Post-termination: applies to former firefighters/peace officers age 65 or younger diagnosed within 15 years of last employment. Tobacco exclusion applies to respiratory tract cancers only if tobacco is a substantial contributing cause. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence showing specific non-occupational cause.

'Peace officer' means full-time peace officer regularly assigned to hazardous duty as part of special operations, SWAT, EOD, or hazmat response unit. Must be 65 or younger and diagnosed within 15 years of last employment. Tobacco exclusion for respiratory tract cancers. Rebuttable by preponderance.

Firefighter-only expanded cancer list (not available to peace officers). Additional requirement: NFPA 1582-aligned physical exam. Must have been exposed to known IARC carcinogen. Former firefighters age 65 or younger diagnosed within 15 years of last employment. Tobacco exclusion for respiratory tract cancers where tobacco is substantial contributing cause.

Event must occur within 24 hours after a known exposure. NFPA 1582-aligned physical exam required. Covers firefighters and volunteer firefighters under § 23-901(6)(d). Tobacco exclusion applies where tobacco is a substantial contributing cause. Rebuttable by preponderance showing specific non-occupational cause.
Volunteer firefighters explicitly covered under § 23-901(6)(d) definition. Event must occur within 24 hours after known exposure. NFPA 1582-aligned physical exam required.

statute_citation: Ariz. Rev. Stat. § 23-972 (conditional on FDA approval of midomafetamine)
date_effective_snippet: 2 conditionally enacted only if FDA approves midomafetamine (MDMA) by December 31, 2025 — FDA did not appro
notes: Employers shall provide WC coverage for firefighters diagnosed with PTSD by a licensed mental health professional with an accepted WC claim. This is a coverage mandate, not a traditional rebuttable presumption. Section 23-972 conditionally enacted only if FDA approves midomafetamine (MDMA) by December 31, 2025 — FDA did not approve as of the 2026 data date; status uncertain. Separate from the counseling program under § 38-673 (which expressly does not create a WC presumption).

statute_citation: Ariz. Rev. Stat. § 23-972 (conditional on FDA approval of midomafetamine)
notes: Covers 'certified peace officers' (§ 38-842). Same conditional enactment tied to FDA approval of midomafetamine by December 31, 2025. Coverage mandate with accepted WC claim, not a traditional shifting presumption.

Covers members of city/county/district fire departments (volunteer, partly paid, fully paid), Department of Forestry and Fire Protection active firefighting members, county forestry/firefighting departments, and warden service of Wildlife Protection Branch. Also covers sheriff's offices, California Highway Patrol, DA investigator staff, and police departments for hernia (not mapped here). Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026. Disease must develop or manifest during service.

Covers members of sheriff's offices, police departments, California Highway Patrol, DA investigator staff, and warden service of Wildlife Protection Branch. Heart trouble and pneumonia only (hernia also included for these groups but is 'other' category). Must develop or manifest during service.
Hernia must develop or manifest during service. Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026.
Covers sheriff's offices, police, CHP, DA investigators. Regular salaried county/city-county peace officers also covered for hernia manifesting during service.

statute_citation: Cal. Lab. Code § 3212.1 (William Dallas Jones Cancer Presumption Act of 2010)
notes: Covers active firefighting members (volunteer, partly paid, fully paid) of city/county/district/state fire departments, UC/CSU, Cal Fire, county forestry; also peace officers (§830.1, §830.2(a), §830.37(a)(b)) primarily in active law enforcement; fire and rescue services coordinators at Cal OES. Member must demonstrate exposure to known IARC carcinogen. Rebuttable if carcinogen not reasonably linked to disabling cancer and primary site established. Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026. Post-termination max is 120 months (10 years) — longer than most other CA presumptions.
Volunteer firefighters explicitly included in § 3212.1(a)(1) definition of covered active firefighting members.

Peace officers defined in § 830.1, § 830.2(a), § 830.37(a)(b) primarily engaged in active law enforcement. Also covered under Government Code § 31720.6 (safety members with 5+ years service, county pension systems).

Covers sheriff's offices, police/fire departments, Cal Fire active firefighting members, county forestry, Penal Code Chapter 4.5 individuals. Blood-borne infectious disease: post-termination window is 3 months/year, max 60 months. MRSA skin infection: separate flat 90-day post-termination window. Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026 (§ SEC. 5 / § 3212.8(e)).
Volunteer fire department members covered ('whether those persons are volunteer, partly paid, or fully paid').
Covers sheriff's offices, police departments, Penal Code Chapter 4.5 individuals. Also covered under Government Code § 31720.7 (county pension system members including probation officers).

date_sunset_snippet: , 'whether volunteers, partly paid, or fully paid'). Sunset: repealed January 1, 2029.
notes: Volunteer firefighters explicitly included (active firefighting members, 'whether volunteers, partly paid, or fully paid'). Sunset: repealed January 1, 2029.

date_sunset_snippet: , 830.5, 830.55 primarily in active law enforcement. Sunset: repealed January 1, 2029.
notes: Peace officers defined in Penal Code §§ 830.1, 830.2(a)(b)(c), 830.32, 830.37(a)(b), 830.5, 830.55 primarily in active law enforcement. Sunset: repealed January 1, 2029.

Covers regular fully-paid fire department members plus active Cal Fire firefighting members. Employer may require pre-employment TB test. Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026. Government Code § 31720.92 provides parallel disability retirement presumption (county pension systems).

Covers full-time salaried police, sheriff, CHP, DA investigators, and prison/jail guards and correctional officers employed by a public agency. Government Code § 31720.92 provides parallel disability retirement presumption (county pension systems).
Prison or jail guards and correctional officers employed by a public agency are explicitly covered under § 3212.6.

Covers regular full-time salaried fire department members and county forestry/firefighting. Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026. Government Code § 31720.93 provides parallel disability retirement presumption.
Covers regular full-time salaried police, sheriff, CHP, county probation officers, DA investigators. Government Code § 31720.93 provides parallel disability retirement presumption.

Covers peace officers (§§ 830.1-830.5) and members of fire departments including volunteers, partly paid, fully paid, Cal Fire, county forestry. Extended to DoD/NASA/FAA airport fire departments for injuries on or after January 1, 2026. Government Code § 31720.9 provides parallel disability retirement presumption.
Volunteers explicitly included in 'members of a fire department' definition under § 3212.85(e)(2).
Peace officers under Penal Code §§ 830.1-830.5. Government Code § 31720.9 provides parallel disability retirement presumption for county pension system peace officer and firefighter members.

statute_citation: Cal. Gov. Code § 7523 (COVID-19 Disability Retirement Presumption)
date_sunset_snippet: Expired: § 7523.2 repealed this article effective January 1, 2024. Covered public ret
notes: Expired: § 7523.2 repealed this article effective January 1, 2024. Covered public retirement system members in firefighter and peace officer job classifications under § 3212.87. Rebuttable presumption of service-related disability for COVID-19-related disability retirement.

statute_citation: Cal. Gov. Code § 7523 (COVID-19 Disability Retirement Presumption)
date_sunset_snippet: Expired: § 7523.2 repealed this article effective January 1, 2024. Covered public ret
notes: Expired: § 7523.2 repealed this article effective January 1, 2024. Covered public retirement system members in peace officer job classifications under § 3212.87.

County pension system (Chapter 4 / Chapter 5 systems). Requires 5 years service. Covers safety members, firefighter members (including active fire suppression members not classified as safety members), and members in active law enforcement. Disease not attributed to pre-existing condition. Rebuttable.
County pension system. 5 years service required. Covers members in active law enforcement.

County pension system disability retirement presumption. Member must have been employed full-time for at least 5 consecutive months in a calendar year in the listed classification AND required to wear a duty belt as a condition of employment.

Applies to full-time firefighters employed minimum 1,600 hours annually. Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal substantial evidence of the condition. Employer may rebut by preponderance of medical evidence.

Requires 5 years continuous full-time employment. Malfunction must occur during or within 48 hours after a qualifying work event (fire suppression, rescue, hazmat, EMS, disaster relief, or other emergency response). Benefits reduced 25% if firefighter smoked tobacco within 5 years preceding event. Benefits offset by WC, pension, Social Security, retirement, and other employer-paid income. Maximum $250,000 per event. This is a benefit statute under Title 29, not a WC presumption under Title 8.

Employer may (not shall) provide equivalent benefits for volunteer firefighters. Requires 5 years continuous service with the same employer. Malfunction must occur during or within 48 hours after a qualifying work event. Benefits reduced 25% if firefighter smoked tobacco within 5 years preceding event.
Baseline test must show employee was uninfected at time of exposure. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence that exposure did not occur on the job.
Statute covers 'emergency services providers.' Baseline test must show employee was uninfected at time of exposure. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence.
Statute covers 'peace officers.' Baseline test must show employee was uninfected at time of exposure. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence.

Applies ONLY to uniformed paid municipal firefighters who began employment BEFORE July 1, 1996. Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of hypertension or heart disease. Benefits provided in lieu of chapter 568 WC benefits for hypertension/heart disease. Includes retirement/survivor benefits. 'Municipal employer' as defined in § 7-467.

Applies ONLY to regular paid municipal police officers who began employment BEFORE July 1, 1996. Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of hypertension or heart disease. Benefits provided in lieu of chapter 568 WC benefits. Includes retirement/survivor benefits.

Applies to uniformed paid municipal firefighters hired after July 1, 1996. Pre-employment exam by licensed physician must have failed to reveal evidence of condition. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

Applies to regular members of paid municipal police departments hired after July 1, 1996. Pre-employment exam by licensed physician must have failed to reveal evidence of condition. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.
Covers uniformed paid and volunteer firefighters. Also includes prostate cancer and testicular cancer under this occupational disease section.
Covers uniformed members of paid and volunteer fire departments. Also includes prostate cancer and testicular cancer under this occupational disease section.
Covers regular paid municipal police officers and constables. Also includes prostate cancer and testicular cancer under this occupational disease section.
Covers members of volunteer ambulance services. Also includes prostate cancer and testicular cancer under this occupational disease section.

date_effective_snippet: tate employers no longer obligated to provide compensation. Effective October 1, 2023.
notes: Requires 5 years as interior structural firefighter or equivalent fire marshal/investigator. Tobacco exclusion: no cigarette use during 15-year period prior to diagnosis. Benefits offset by other benefits received. If firefighters cancer relief account becomes insolvent, municipal/state employers no longer obligated to provide compensation. Effective October 1, 2023.
Qualifying events include witnessing death, traumatic injury, or COVID-19 exposure. Medical records subject to workers' compensation guidelines.
Qualifying events include witnessing death, traumatic injury, or COVID-19 exposure.
Covers emergency medical services personnel. Qualifying events include witnessing death, traumatic injury, or COVID-19 exposure.
Covers Department of Correction employees and telecommunicators. Qualifying events include witnessing death, traumatic injury, or COVID-19 exposure.

Covers sworn firefighters with DC Fire and EMS Department. Presumption applies when diagnosis results in inability to perform full duties or death. Pre-employment exam must have shown freedom from condition. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence to the contrary. Coverage denied if eligible vaccine refused without physician-documented health risk (§ 5-655). Eligibility requires compliance with annual physical examinations (§ 5-655.01).
Covers non-sworn EMS employees of DC Fire and EMS Department. Presumption applies when diagnosis results in inability to perform full duties or death. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.
Covers sworn firefighters with DC Fire and EMS Department. Presumption applies when diagnosis results in inability to perform full duties or death. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.
Covers non-sworn EMS employees of DC Fire and EMS Department. Presumption applies when diagnosis results in inability to perform full duties or death. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

Covers sworn firefighters. Requires 10 years of service. Second cancer category (brain, non-Hodgkin's, throat, kidney, thyroid, bladder) requires documented toxic substance exposure. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.
Covers non-sworn EMS employees. Requires 10 years of service. Second cancer category requires documented toxic substance exposure. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

Requires documented exposure to blood or bodily fluids. Covers sworn firefighters. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence. Coverage denied if eligible vaccine refused without physician-documented health risk (§ 5-655).
Requires documented exposure to blood or bodily fluids. Covers non-sworn EMS employees. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

Requires 3 years of active service (1,250 hours/year for paid members; active status per department for volunteers). Statute is a benefit fund, not a WC presumption — no rebuttability language. Benefits: early-stage $5,000 lump sum; advanced-stage $20,000 lump sum; combined lifetime max $40,000. Monthly disability: Class 1 (paid active) = lesser of 60% salary or $4,000/month; Class 2 (other eligible) = $1,500/month; up to 36 months duration. Paid and volunteer firefighters both covered; also covers EMTs, paramedics, fire instructors, fire marshals, fire police.

Volunteer active status defined per individual department. Requires 3 years active service. Monthly disability benefit for Class 2: $1,500/month up to 36 months. Statute explicitly covers volunteer firefighters.

Statute explicitly covers EMTs and paramedics. Requires 3 years active service (1,250 hours/year for paid members). Monthly disability benefit for Class 1 (paid active) = lesser of 60% salary or $4,000/month; Class 2 (other eligible) = $1,500/month.

Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumption that condition was accidental and suffered in the line of duty. Does not apply to life/disability insurance unless negotiated.
Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumption that condition was accidental and suffered in the line of duty.
Covers law enforcement officers and correctional officers as defined in s. 943.10(1), (2), or (3). Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal the condition.
Covers law enforcement officers and correctional officers as defined in s. 943.10(1), (2), or (3). Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal the condition.

Employee must file written affidavit declaring no outside exposure. Pre-employment exam required (testing for hepatitis/TB; meningitis exempt from exam requirement). Employer must maintain exposure records. Immunization refusal disqualifies from presumption. Covers full-time employees only; excludes public hospital employees.

Covers paramedics and EMTs employed full-time by the state or political subdivision. Written affidavit of no outside exposure required. Immunization refusal disqualifies. Excludes public hospital employees.
Covers law enforcement and correctional officers employed full-time. Written affidavit of no outside exposure required. Immunization refusal disqualifies. Excludes public hospital employees.

date_effective_snippet: quired. Not subject to apportionment for pre-existing PTSD. Effective October 1, 2018. Also covers volunt
notes: PTSD is a compensable occupational disease. Must be diagnosed by a licensed psychiatrist who is an authorized treating physician. Must result from one of enumerated qualifying events (e.g., death of a minor, grievous bodily harm). No physical injury required. Not subject to apportionment for pre-existing PTSD. Effective October 1, 2018. Also covers volunteer firefighters.

date_effective_snippet: enumerated qualifying events. No physical injury required. Effective October 1, 2018. Also covers volunt
notes: PTSD is a compensable occupational disease for EMTs and paramedics. Must be diagnosed by a licensed psychiatrist. Must result from enumerated qualifying events. No physical injury required. Effective October 1, 2018. Also covers volunteer EMTs/paramedics.

date_effective_snippet: enumerated qualifying events. No physical injury required. Effective October 1, 2018. Also covers volunt
notes: PTSD is a compensable occupational disease for law enforcement officers. Must be diagnosed by a licensed psychiatrist. Must result from enumerated qualifying events. No physical injury required. Effective October 1, 2018. Also covers volunteer law enforcement.

date_effective_snippet: irefighter. This is an alternative to WC under chapter 440. Effective July 1, 2019.
notes: Covers full-time firefighters only (not volunteer). Must not have used tobacco products for at least 5 preceding years. Must not have been employed in a higher-risk position in the preceding 5 years. Benefits include employer-sponsored health plan coverage (with out-of-pocket reimbursement) and $25,000 one-time cash payout on initial diagnosis. Post-termination coverage available for 10 years if eligibility criteria met at termination and not subsequently employed as firefighter. This is an alternative to WC under chapter 440. Effective July 1, 2019.

date_effective_snippet: ly disability benefit) rather than a direct WC presumption. Effective January 1, 2018. Volunteers covered
notes: Requires 12 consecutive months of service as a firefighter with the same fire department. Provides mandatory insurance coverage (lump sum + monthly disability benefit) rather than a direct WC presumption. Effective January 1, 2018. Volunteers covered under same statute but with lower benefit levels ($1,500/month vs. 60% of salary).

date_effective_snippet: efit is $1,500 (vs. 60% of salary for career firefighters). Effective January 1, 2018. Volunteer fire dep
notes: Volunteers explicitly included in the cancer insurance mandate. Monthly disability benefit is $1,500 (vs. 60% of salary for career firefighters). Effective January 1, 2018. Volunteer fire departments not required to cover volunteers who are simultaneously employed as career firefighters at another department.

Presumption applies if heart attack, stroke, or vascular rupture: commenced while performing work-related activity, while on duty after such activity, or not later than 24 hours after such activity; AND directly or proximately resulted in death or disability. Rebuttable by competent medical evidence that it was not related to work activity or was caused solely by cardiovascular disease risk factors.

Statute covers 'public safety officers' defined to include emergency medical technicians. Presumption applies to heart attack, stroke, or vascular rupture within 24 hours of work-related activity. Rebuttable by competent medical evidence.

Statute covers 'public safety officers' defined to include law enforcement officers. Presumption applies to heart attack, stroke, or vascular rupture within 24 hours of work-related activity. Rebuttable by competent medical evidence.

Statute covers 'public safety officers' defined to include prison guards. Presumption applies to heart attack, stroke, or vascular rupture within 24 hours of work-related activity. Rebuttable by competent medical evidence.

Applies for service-connected disability retirement. Presumption requires that entry physical exam failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Inhalation of smoke, toxic gases, chemical fumes, and other toxic vapors shall be construed as injury received in performance of duty.

Applies for service-connected disability retirement. Presumption requires that entry physical exam failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Inhalation of smoke, toxic gases, chemical fumes construed as occupational hazard.
Statute explicitly covers police officers. Presumption requires that entry physical exam failed to reveal any evidence of the condition.
Statute explicitly covers police officers. Presumption requires that entry physical exam failed to reveal any evidence of the condition.

statute_citation: Haw. Rev. Stat. § 386 (medical care for firefighters with cancer)
notes: When a cancer claim is accepted as compensable for a firefighter with 5+ years of service, employer is liable for medical care at 110%-150% of Medicare RBRVS fees applicable to Hawaii. Provision is about enhanced medical benefit level, not a WC presumption per se, but applies once claim is accepted or determined compensable.

Presumed contracted while on active duty as a result of strain. Pre-existing conditions known at membership commencement (on or after July 1, 1992) are excluded. Applies only to Chapter 411 retirement system members (police and firefighters in cities). Covers firefighters who passed regular civil service examination.
Presumed contracted while on active duty as result of inhalation of noxious fumes, poison, or gases. Pre-existing conditions known at membership commencement (on or after July 1, 1992) are excluded.
Cancer defined by § 411.1(6). Presumed contracted while on active duty as a result of that duty. Pre-existing conditions known at membership commencement (on or after July 1, 1992) are excluded.

Infectious disease defined by § 411.1(11). Presumed contracted while on active duty as a result of that duty. Pre-existing conditions known at membership commencement (on or after July 1, 1992) are excluded.

Covers paid firemen employed by a municipality, village or fire district as regular members. Caused by overexertion in times of stress or danger OR by proximate exposure OR cumulative exposure over 4+ years to heat, smoke, chemical fumes or toxic gases. Applies to paid firefighters only (not volunteers). Covered diseases are not subject to the limitations of § 72-439.

Covers paid firemen employed by municipality, village or fire district as regular members. Caused by overexertion or cumulative exposure over 4+ years to heat, smoke, chemical fumes or toxic gases. Paid firefighters only.
Applies to occupations involving exposure to human blood or body fluids, which encompasses firefighters. No specific service minimum stated for this provision.

Each cancer type has its own minimum years-of-service threshold (5-15 years). 'Firefighter' = employee whose primary duty is extinguishing or investigating fires. Pre-employment medical screening exam must not have revealed the disease. Presumption is rebuttable by substantial evidence. Post-termination window is 10 years (120 months) from last date worked. Tobacco exclusion: presumption does not apply if firefighter or firefighter's cohabitant has regularly and habitually used tobacco products for 10+ years prior to diagnosis. Testicular cancer: age <40 and no evidence of anabolic steroids or human growth hormone use. Breast cancer: age <40 without breast cancer genetic predisposition.

date_sunset_snippet: event or events arising out of employment. Originally had a sunset of July 1, 2023 (Section 2 of the a
notes: Requires diagnosis by a psychologist, licensed psychiatrist, or counselor trained in PTSI; and clear and convincing evidence the injury was caused by an event or events arising out of employment. Originally had a sunset of July 1, 2023 (Section 2 of the act), but effective for dates of injury/manifestation on or after July 1, 2019. Applies to firefighters as defined in §§ 59-1391(f) and 72-1403(A).

date_sunset_snippet: agnosis and clear and convincing evidence. Originally had a sunset of July 1, 2023. Effective for date
notes: Applies to peace officers (city, county, Idaho state police) as defined in § 19-5101(d). Requires PTSI diagnosis and clear and convincing evidence. Originally had a sunset of July 1, 2023. Effective for dates of injury/manifestation on or after July 1, 2019.

date_sunset_snippet: agnosis and clear and convincing evidence. Originally had a sunset of July 1, 2023. Effective for date
notes: Applies to EMS providers certified under §§ 56-1011 through 56-1018B and ambulance-based clinicians. Requires PTSI diagnosis and clear and convincing evidence. Originally had a sunset of July 1, 2023. Effective for dates of injury/manifestation on or after July 1, 2019.

statute_citation: 40 ILCS 5/4-110.1 (municipalities 500,000 and under); 40 ILCS 5/6-151.1 (cities over 500,000); 820 ILCS 305 (Workers' Compensation Act); 820 ILCS 310 (Workers' Occupational Diseases Act)
notes: Pension provisions (40 ILCS 5/4-110.1 and 6-151.1) require 5 years (Article 4) or 7 years (Article 6) of service, active status, and disease to manifest during service. Article 4 applies to municipalities 500K and under; Article 6 to cities over 500K (7-year minimum). WC/OD Acts (820 ILCS 305/310) require 5 years employment at time of filing Application for Adjustment of Claim. Pre-employment exam required under pension provisions: if exam shows impairment, firefighter loses entitlement until subsequent exam shows no impairment.
Tuberculosis and lung/respiratory disease covered under all four statutory provisions. Article 4 (5-year minimum), Article 6 (7-year minimum). Pre-employment exam required under pension provisions.

Cancer type must be one that may be caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or a known IARC carcinogen (4-110.1 and WC/OD Acts). Pre-employment exam: if exam discloses existence of cancer, firefighter loses entitlement until subsequent exam shows no cancer. Article 6 (cities over 500K) requires 7 years.

AIDS and hepatitis C covered in Article 6 (cities over 500K; 7-year minimum) and WC/OD Acts. MRSA covered under all three WC/OD-related provisions. Pre-employment exam required under Article 6: if exam discloses AIDS, hepatitis C, or MRSA, firefighter loses entitlement until subsequent exam shows no impairment.
Hernia and hearing loss explicitly included in the rebuttable presumption under both the Workers' Compensation Act and the Workers' Occupational Diseases Act.

WC/OD Acts cover EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Exclusion: presumption does NOT apply to EMT/A-EMT/paramedic employed by a private employer if the employee spends the preponderance of work time engaged in medical transfers between medical care facilities or non-emergency medical transfers.
Covers EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Private-employer/medical-transfer exclusion applies.
Covers EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Private-employer/medical-transfer exclusion applies.
Covers EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Private-employer/medical-transfer exclusion applies.
Hernia and hearing loss explicitly included for EMT, EMT-I, A-EMT, and paramedic. Private-employer/medical-transfer exclusion applies.

Section 4-112 provides special protections for firefighters receiving disability pensions for PTSD: mandatory annual medical re-examination may be discontinued if firefighter has reached age 45, has documentation from 2+ physicians approving discontinuance, and board votes affirmatively. Also, no physical or mental disability forming the basis of a pension application may be used as cause for discharge by municipalities or fire protection districts.

Applies to full-time employees of state or political subdivision. Tobacco exclusion: chapter does not apply to any individual who used tobacco products in any form in the 5 years before diagnosis. At time of diagnosis: must be actively employed OR have terminated employment not more than 60 months earlier. Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence.
Full-time firefighters only. Tobacco exclusion: chapter does not apply to individuals who used tobacco in the 5 years before diagnosis. Rebuttable by competent evidence.
Full-time firefighters only. Tobacco exclusion applies (no tobacco use in 5 years before diagnosis). Rebuttable by competent evidence.

Parkinson's disease explicitly added as a presumptive condition (IC 5-10-15-5.5 added by P.L.59-2009). Must be caused by toxin or head trauma known to increase Parkinson's risk AND to which individual is at risk for occupational exposure. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.
Applies to full-time emergency medical services providers as defined in IC 16-41-10-1. Tobacco exclusion: no tobacco use in the 5 years before diagnosis. Rebuttable by competent evidence.
Full-time EMS providers. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.
Full-time EMS providers. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.
Full-time EMS providers. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.
Applies to full-time members of a police department as defined in IC 36-8-1-9. Tobacco exclusion: no tobacco use in the 5 years before diagnosis. Rebuttable by competent evidence.
Full-time law enforcement. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.
Full-time law enforcement. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.
Full-time law enforcement. Tobacco exclusion applies. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

Applies to 1977 Fund members (firefighters under IC 36-8-8). Mental health disability treated as Class 1 impairment (same as occupational disease). Mental health disability review panel process: initial 2-year provisional period with required active treatment; panel reevaluates at conclusion; second 2-year provisional period if still impaired; permanent disability after second evaluation if still impaired. Applies to final determinations after December 31, 2012. Employer pays for mental health care during both provisional periods. This is a pension/disability benefit structure, not a WC presumption per se.

Applies to 1977 Fund members (police officers and firefighters under IC 36-8-8). Review panel includes active or retired police officer or firefighter depending on the fund member being evaluated. Same provisional period structure as for firefighters. Employer pays for mental health care during provisional periods.

Presumption applies after 5 years of credited service. If member ceased being a contributing member due to service-connected disability for 6+ months and then rejoined, the presumption for heart/lung/cancer does not apply again until re-contributing for 2 years or clear evidence of duty-connection. Presumption is part of pension/retirement system (KPERS), not standalone WC statute. Burden shifts to member or other party once employer presents contrary evidence.

Same statute covers 'policeman or fire fighter.' Presumption applies after 5 years of credited service. Re-entry restriction after service-connected disability gap of 6+ months (must re-contribute 2 years before presumption re-activates). Part of KPERS pension system.
Presumption applies after 5 years of credited service. Same re-entry restriction as cardiovascular row. Part of KPERS pension system.
Same statute covers 'policeman or fire fighter.' Presumption applies after 5 years of credited service. Part of KPERS pension system.
Cancer presumption limited to types that 'may, in general, result from exposure to heat, radiation, or a known carcinogen.' Same re-entry restriction applies. Part of KPERS pension system.
Same statute covers 'policeman or fire fighter.' Cancer limited to types resulting from heat, radiation, or known carcinogen exposure. Part of KPERS pension system.

CRITICAL: This provision creates a line-of-duty DEATH BENEFIT presumption only — the statute explicitly states it does NOT create a WC presumption under KRS Chapter 342 and shall not be construed to do so. Conditions: firefighter for at least 5 consecutive years; cancer-related death within 10 years of separation; no tobacco use for 10 years prior to diagnosis; under age 65 at time of death; no pre-employment cancer diagnosis; exposed to IARC or NTP recognized carcinogen reasonably associated with the cancer. Rebuttability not explicitly stated for this benefit.

Covers full-time, career, and volunteer firefighters. Diagnosis must be by psychiatrist, psychologist, or licensed professional counselor (KRS 335.500–335.599). PTSD must be caused by an event or accumulation of events in course and scope of employment. NOTE: This provision is a mental health treatment reimbursement program (out-of-pocket costs reimbursed by commission), not a traditional WC compensability presumption — maximum 12 months of reimbursement. Personnel action exclusion applies (transfer, promotion, termination not compensable).

Explicitly covers volunteer firefighters. Same diagnosis and reimbursement structure as career firefighters. Mental health treatment reimbursement program, not WC compensability presumption. Maximum 12 months reimbursement.

Covers volunteer members of fire companies under WC coverage per R.S. 23:1036. Diagnosis must be by psychiatrist or psychologist. PTSI may be diagnosed during or after volunteer service. Prima facie presumption of service-connection. Personnel action exclusion (transfer, promotion, demotion, termination not compensable).

Covers firefighters in classified service with 2+ years of service. Rebuttable by: (1) evidence meeting judicial standards, or (2) evidence that within 1 year of diagnosis member unlawfully used IV controlled substances, or lived in intimate relationship with a person diagnosed with Hep B or C.
Explicitly covers 'policeman in the classified service' with 2+ years of service alongside firefighters. Same rebuttability conditions as firefighter row.

Covers firefighters in classified service with 10+ years. Cancer limited to types caused by heat, smoke, radiation, or IARC-recognized known/suspected carcinogens. Two-tier post-termination window: pre-2023 terminations get 3 months/year up to 60 months; post-2023 terminations get 120 months for qualifying members (long service + age criteria). Age 65 cap on diagnosis for post-2023 terminations.

Covers classified fire service. Presumption is prima facie for disease/infirmity manifested any time after the first 5 years of employment. No explicit post-termination window stated. Member need not be on duty when stricken. Statute is separate from and does not affect R.S. 33:2011 (cancer) provisions.

Same statute as cardiovascular row (R.S. 33:2581). Covers lung disease alongside heart disease in classified fire service. Presumption is prima facie after 5 years of employment. No explicit post-termination window stated.

Covers classified fire service. Hearing loss must be 10% greater than the affected employee's comparable age group in the general population. Entitlement is to medical benefits (including hearing prosthesis) only — not full disability benefits. Baseline audiology exam required for new hires on or after July 1, 2006 (within 1 year of appointment). Presumption applies after 5 years of service. 24-month post-termination window.

Covers 'fire employee' (full-time regularly paid fire department employment) and volunteer firemen. Diagnosis must be by psychiatrist or psychologist. PTSI may be diagnosed during or after employment. Prima facie presumption of service-connection. Personnel action exclusion applies. Does not modify pension/retirement eligibility calculations.

Explicitly covers 'emergency medical services personnel' as defined in R.S. 40:1075.3 employed pursuant to this Chapter. Same prima facie presumption structure as firefighters. Diagnosis during or after employment. Personnel action exclusion applies.
Explicitly covers 'employee of a police department' as defined in R.S. 33:2211. Same prima facie presumption structure. Diagnosis during or after employment. Personnel action exclusion applies.

Covers employees of the Louisiana Division of State Police (separate statute from R.S. 33:2581.2 which covers municipal police). Diagnosis by psychiatrist or psychologist. PTSI may be diagnosed during or after employment. Prima facie presumption for WC purposes. Personnel action exclusion applies. Does not modify pension/retirement eligibility.

Covers uniformed members of paid fire departments, including permanent crash crewmen, crash boatmen, fire controlmen, and assistant fire controlmen at Logan International Airport. Pre-employment or subsequent physical exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumption rebuttable by competent evidence.

Covers permanent members of police departments (including park police), MBTA police force, state police, and public works building police. Pre-employment or subsequent physical exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumption rebuttable by competent evidence.

Covers employees in the Department of Correction or county correctional facility whose regular or incidental duties require care, supervision, or custody of prisoners, criminally insane persons, or defective delinquents. Pre-employment or subsequent physical exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumption rebuttable by competent evidence.

Covers uniformed members of paid fire departments, including crash crewmen, crash boatmen, fire controlmen, and assistant fire controlmen at Logan International Airport. Must result in total disability or death. Presumed to result from inhalation of noxious fumes or poisonous gases. Pre-employment or subsequent physical exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of such condition. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

Covers uniformed members of paid fire departments, members of state police assigned to fire investigation unit of Department of Fire Services, state police K-9 unit members, and Logan Airport crash crewmen/boatmen/fire controlmen. Must result in total disability or death. Requires: (1) 5 years minimum service; (2) cancer type must be one that may result from heat, radiation, or IARC-listed carcinogen; (3) member must have regularly responded to fire calls or investigation at scene. Pre-employment or subsequent exam must not have revealed the condition. Post-termination window: discovery within 5 years of last active service date. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence of non-service-connected risk factors or hazards.

date_effective_snippet: mulative service, and currently employed at time of claim — effective October 1, 2026.
notes: Covers paid firefighters, paid fire fighting instructors, sworn members of Office of State Fire Marshal employed by airport authority, county, fire control district, municipality, or State. Hypertension sub-provision (§9-503(a)(2)) requires: hypertension diagnosis by authorized provider, medication prescribed for at least 90 consecutive days, at least 2 years cumulative service, and currently employed at time of claim — effective October 1, 2026.
Covers paid firefighters, paid fire fighting instructors, sworn members of Office of State Fire Marshal employed by airport authority, county, fire control district, municipality, or State.

date_effective_snippet: mulative service, and currently employed at time of claim — effective October 1, 2026.
notes: Covers paid rescue squad members and paid advanced life support unit members. Hypertension sub-provision (§9-503(a)(2)) requires: diagnosis by authorized provider, medication for at least 90 consecutive days, at least 2 years cumulative service, and currently employed at time of claim — effective October 1, 2026.
Covers paid rescue squad members and paid advanced life support unit members.

Covers volunteer firefighters, volunteer fire fighting instructors, volunteer rescue squad members, and volunteer advanced life support unit members who are covered employees under § 9-234. Pre-employment physical exam required. Hypertension expanded provision (§9-503(a)(2)) applies only to paid members.
Covers volunteer rescue squad members and volunteer advanced life support unit members who are covered employees under § 9-234. Pre-employment physical exam required.

Requires: (1) cancer caused by contact with toxic substance encountered in the line of duty; (2) at least 10 years of service as firefighter, fire fighting instructor, rescue squad member, or advanced life support unit member; (3) unable to perform normal duties due to cancer. Covers paid firefighters, paid fire fighting instructors, and sworn members of Office of State Fire Marshal.

Covers volunteer firefighters, volunteer fire fighting instructors, volunteer rescue squad members, and volunteer advanced life support unit members who are covered employees under § 9-234. Pre-employment physical exam required. Requires 10 years of service; cancer must be caused by toxic substance in line of duty; must be unable to perform normal duties.

Covers active members of municipal fire departments AND volunteer firefighters associations. Firefighter defined broadly to include administrative/other duty members who aid in fire extinguishment. 2-year membership minimum required. Temporal trigger: disease/injury within 6 months of actual firefighting or firefighting training/drill.
Volunteer firefighters association members explicitly included in 'firefighter' definition under Sec. 328. Same 2-year membership and 6-month temporal trigger requirements.
Same statute as cardiovascular row (Sec. 328). Pulmonary disease covered alongside cardiovascular. Same 2-year minimum and 6-month firefighting-activity trigger.
Volunteer firefighters association members explicitly included. Same conditions as career firefighter row.

Covers 'emergency rescue or public safety workers' with high risk of occupational exposure. Firefighter defined as active member of municipal fire department or volunteer fire association. Pre-employment physical required (if employer requires it) — must test negative for hepatitis/TB. Worker must sign affidavit declaring no outside exposure sources, no transfusions, no high-risk sexual behavior, no IV drug use. Preemployment test exemption for workers employed on the effective date of the section. Multi-employer rule: last employer of injurious exposure is liable. Presumption explicitly applies for disability retirement purposes.

Volunteer fire association members explicitly included in firefighter definition under Sec. 328-A. Employer includes entities for which person provides volunteer services. Same affidavit, pre-employment testing, and immunization requirements.

EMS person defined as licensed under Title 32, ch. 2-B, employed by or providing voluntary service to a licensed ambulance service or nontransporting EMS. Must run high risk of occupational exposure. Same affidavit, pre-employment testing, and immunization conditions.
Law enforcement officer as defined in Title 25, sec. 2801-A, sub. 5. Must run high risk of occupational exposure. Same affidavit, pre-employment testing, and immunization conditions.

Corrections officer as defined in Title 25, sec. 2801-A, sub. 2. Must run high risk of occupational exposure through custody/restraint duties. Same affidavit, pre-employment testing, and immunization conditions.

Covers municipal fire department members and volunteer fire association members (via 'employed' definition that includes volunteer members with no compensation other than injury/death benefits). Minimum 5 years employment; must have regularly responded to firefighting or emergency calls (exception for State Fire Marshal investigators/sergeants and forest protection employees). Pre-employment test required (must test negative for cancer). Firefighter must sign affidavit that cancer is not prevalent in blood-related family and no substantial lifetime carcinogen exposure other than through firefighting. State Fire Marshal investigators/sergeants must certify use of protective equipment per departmental policies.

'Employed' definition in Sec. 328-B explicitly includes volunteer fire association members with no compensation other than injury/death benefits. Same 5-year service, pre-employment test, affidavit, and post-termination window requirements.

Rebuttable by clear and convincing evidence. Diagnosis must be by allopathic or osteopathic physician (Title 32, ch. 48 or 36) with psychiatry specialization, or licensed psychologist (Title 32, ch. 56). Personnel action exclusion: PTSD not compensable if resulting from disciplinary action, work evaluation, job transfer, layoff, demotion, termination, or similar good-faith employer action. Firefighter defined as in Sec. 328-A.
Law enforcement officer as defined in Sec. 328-A (Title 25, sec. 2801-A). Same diagnosis and rebuttability requirements. Personnel action exclusion applies.
Corrections officer as defined in Sec. 328-A (Title 25, sec. 2801-A). Same diagnosis and rebuttability requirements. Personnel action exclusion applies.
Emergency medical services worker as defined in Sec. 328-A. Same diagnosis and rebuttability requirements. Personnel action exclusion applies.

statute_citation: Michigan Worker's Disability Compensation Act of 1969, Act 317, § 418.405(1)
notes: Covers full-time members of fully paid fire departments (city, township, village, airport county/public authority/state university, or public fire authority). Disease must develop or manifest during active service. Presumption applies in absence of evidence to the contrary (§418.405(3)). As condition precedent, claimant must first apply for pension benefits or demonstrate ineligibility.

statute_citation: Michigan Worker's Disability Compensation Act of 1969, Act 317, § 418.405(1)
notes: Covers full-time members of fully paid fire departments (city, township, village, airport county/public authority/state university, or public fire authority). Disease must develop or manifest during active service. Presumption applies in absence of evidence to the contrary (§418.405(3)). As condition precedent, claimant must first apply for pension benefits or demonstrate ineligibility.

statute_citation: Michigan Worker's Disability Compensation Act of 1969, Act 317, § 418.405(1)
notes: Covers full-time members of fully paid police departments (city, township, village), county sheriffs and deputies, members of state police, conservation officers, and motor carrier enforcement division officers. Disease must develop or manifest during active service. Presumption applies in absence of evidence to the contrary. Pension application required as condition precedent.

statute_citation: Michigan Worker's Disability Compensation Act of 1969, Act 317, § 418.405(1)
notes: Covers full-time members of fully paid police departments, county sheriffs and deputies, state police members, conservation officers, and motor carrier enforcement division officers. Disease must develop or manifest during active service. Presumption applies in absence of evidence to the contrary. Pension application required as condition precedent.

statute_citation: Michigan Worker's Disability Compensation Act of 1969, Act 317, § 418.405(2)
notes: Covers full-time fire department members (all service lengths) and, beginning January 1, 2022 for cancers diagnosed on or after January 1, 2022, part-time, paid on-call, and volunteer members. Also covers former members from any of those categories. Requires 60 months (5 years) active service at time of cancer manifestation. Member must have been exposed to hazards incidental to fire suppression, rescue, or EMS duties. Claim is made against the Christopher R. Slezak First Responder Presumed Coverage Fund (not the employer) — employer claim is suspended during fund benefit receipt. Presumption applies only to claims against the Fund. Tobacco rebuttal: rebuttable by scientific evidence that claimant was a substantial and consistent user of tobacco products within 10 years preceding injury and that use was a significant factor in cancer cause/aggravation/progression. Pension application required as condition precedent.

statute_citation: Michigan Worker's Disability Compensation Act of 1969, Act 317, § 418.405(2)
notes: Volunteer members (and former volunteer members) eligible for cancer presumption beginning January 1, 2022 for cancers diagnosed on or after January 1, 2022. Requires 60 months (5 years) active service at time of cancer manifestation. Exposure to fire suppression/rescue/EMS hazards required. Claims go against the Christopher R. Slezak First Responder Presumed Coverage Fund; fund is considered employer for volunteer members. Forest fire officers and fire/crash rescue officers also covered for same cancer list. Tobacco rebuttal provision applies. Pension application required as condition precedent.

Covers active duty members of organized fire departments of any municipality. Pre-employment thorough physical examination by licensed physician required; written report must be filed with department; exam must have been negative for the disease at time of employment. Disease must manifest at date of disablement or death while employed on active duty. Statute uses 'presumptively an occupational disease' language with no express rebuttal provision for this subsection — treated as irrebuttable here.

Covers active duty members of organized fire departments of any municipality. Pre-employment thorough physical examination by licensed physician required; written report must be filed with department; exam must have been negative for the disease. Disease must manifest at date of disablement or death while employed on active duty. No express rebuttal provision for this subsection.

Covers active duty members of organized police departments of any municipality, Minnesota State Patrol members, conservation officers, state crime bureau members, forest officers (DNR), state correctional officers, and sheriffs/full-time deputy sheriffs. Pre-employment physical exam required; written report filed; exam must have been negative. No express rebuttal provision for this subsection.

Same coverage and conditions as firefighter respiratory row. Covers state patrol, police, conservation officers, crime bureau, DNR forest officers, correctional officers, sheriffs/deputies. Pre-employment physical exam required; written report filed; exam must have been negative. No express rebuttal provision.

Covers licensed firefighters. Disease must be contracted through exposure in course of employment outside of a hospital. Rebuttable by substantial factors brought by employer or insurer; any substantial factors known at time of denial must be communicated to employee on denial of liability.

Covers paramedics and emergency medical technicians. Covers individuals who by nature of their position provide emergency medical care, including licensed nurses providing emergency care. Disease must be contracted through exposure in course of employment outside of a hospital. Rebuttable by substantial factors; known factors must be communicated to employee on denial.

Covers licensed police officers under § 626.84, subd. 1. Disease must be contracted through exposure in course of employment outside of a hospital. Rebuttable by substantial factors; known factors must be communicated to employee on denial.

Covers state correctional officers. Disease must be contracted through exposure in course of employment outside of a hospital. Rebuttable by substantial factors; known factors must be communicated to employee on denial.

Covers firefighters on active duty with an organized fire department who are unable to perform duties due to disabling cancer. Cancer type must be caused by exposure to heat, radiation, or IARC-listed carcinogen, and the carcinogen must be reasonably linked to the disabling cancer. Exception: if firefighter entered service after August 1, 1988, and pre-hire exam disclosed existing cancer, presumption does not apply unless subsequent medical determination finds cancer no longer present.

Mental impairment defined as PTSD diagnosis by licensed psychiatrist or psychologist per current DSM. Employee must not have been previously diagnosed with the condition. PTSD is NOT an occupational disease if resulting from: disciplinary action, work evaluation, job transfer, layoff, demotion, promotion, termination, retirement, or similar good-faith employer action. Rebuttable by substantial factors; known factors must be communicated to employee on denial.

Covers paramedics, emergency medical technicians, and licensed nurses employed to provide emergency medical services outside of a medical facility. Also covers public safety dispatchers. Must not have been previously diagnosed with the condition. PTSD excluded if resulting from disciplinary or good-faith employer action. Rebuttable by substantial factors.

Covers licensed police officers and members of the Minnesota State Patrol and full-time deputy sheriffs/sheriffs. Must not have been previously diagnosed. PTSD excluded if resulting from disciplinary or good-faith employer action. Rebuttable by substantial factors.

Covers officers employed by the state or a political subdivision at a corrections, detention, or secure treatment facility. Must not have been previously diagnosed. PTSD excluded if resulting from disciplinary or good-faith employer action. Rebuttable by substantial factors.

§ 87.005 applies only to chapter 87 RSMo 1959 retirement/relief provisions; § 87.006 applies to all county/city/town/fire district paid fire department members for retirement benefit computation. Both require pre-employment exam within 5 years prior to claim. Annual blood test required for infectious disease presumption.
§ 87.005 applies to chapter 87 retirement provisions only; § 87.006 applies to all paid fire departments statewide. Both require pre-employment exam within 5 years.

§ 87.005 covers hypertension and heart disease; § 87.006 additionally covers hypotension. § 87.005 applies to chapter 87 retirement provisions; § 87.006 applies to all paid fire departments statewide.

Tobacco exclusion: presumption defeated if proven to reasonable medical certainty that condition was not contributed to by voluntary tobacco use. Applies to uniformed members of paid fire departments of all counties, cities, towns, fire districts, and other governmental units. § 320.400 establishes a separate voluntary firefighter cancer benefits pool (not a WC presumption; benefits reduced 25% for tobacco use within 5 years of diagnosis).

statute_citation: Mississippi First Responders Health and Safety Act ("Arson Investigator Danny Benton and Police Chief Henry Manuel, Sr., Act"), Section 2-7; Mississippi Code § 71-3-9
date_effective_snippet: Effective July 1, 2021. This is a separate
notes: Effective July 1, 2021. This is a separate cancer benefits program, structured as an alternative to workers' compensation (§71-3-9 amended so employer not liable under WC if first responder elects this Act). Benefits include lump sums ($35,000 for malignant tumors meeting specific criteria; $6,250 for carcinoma in situ or endoscopic-treated malignancies; maximum $50,000 lifetime), plus disability benefit of 60% of monthly salary or $5,000/month max for nonvolunteers, beginning 6 months after disability, for 36 monthly payments. Requires a board-certified physician in the appropriate specialty to certify cancer was caused by an occupational hazard. Employer bears cost; claimant choosing this program forfeits WC claim for same diagnosis. Covers full-time firefighters with 10+ years of service employed by State of Mississippi or any political subdivision.

statute_citation: Mississippi First Responders Health and Safety Act ("Arson Investigator Danny Benton and Police Chief Henry Manuel, Sr., Act"), Section 2-7; Mississippi Code § 71-3-9
notes: Effective July 1, 2021. Covers volunteer firefighters registered with the State of Mississippi or a political subdivision with 10+ years of service. Disability benefit for volunteers is $1,500/month (not 60% of salary), beginning 6 months after disability, for 36 monthly payments. Same lump-sum benefit structure applies ($35,000/$6,250, max $50,000 lifetime). Board-certified physician must certify occupational causation. Electing this program forfeits WC claim for same diagnosis.

statute_citation: Mississippi First Responders Health and Safety Act ("Arson Investigator Danny Benton and Police Chief Henry Manuel, Sr., Act"), Section 2-7; Mississippi Code § 71-3-9
notes: Effective July 1, 2021. Covers law enforcement officers certified by the Mississippi Board on Law Enforcement Officer Standards and Training (BLEOST) with 10+ years of service. Same benefit structure as firefighters: lump-sum up to $50,000 lifetime, disability benefit of 60% of salary or $5,000/month max for 36 months beginning 6 months after disability. Board-certified physician must certify occupational causation. Electing this program forfeits WC claim for same diagnosis.

date_effective_snippet: be the type that can be caused by firefighting activities. Effective July 1, 2019; applies to disease
notes: Tobacco exclusion: firefighter may not be a regular user of tobacco products, must not have a history of regular tobacco use in 10 years preceding claim, and must not have been exposed by a cohabitant who regularly used tobacco in the home for 10+ years before diagnosis. Employment history calculated from July 1, 2014 only (§ 39-71(3)). Volunteer firefighters covered only if employer opts in AND insurer agrees to provide presumptive coverage (§ 39-71(1)(b)). Total claim payment capped at $5 million per claim. Pre-employment exam required within 90 days of hiring; subsequent exams at least every 2 years. Lung cancer and myocardial infarction must be the type that can be caused by firefighting activities. Effective July 1, 2019; applies to diseases diagnosed on or after that date.

Myocardial infarction listed in the statute alongside cancers as a presumptive occupational disease. Must be the type that can be caused by firefighting activities. Same tobacco exclusion and exam requirements as cancer rows. Volunteer firefighters covered only if employer opts in. Employment history calculated from July 1, 2014 only.

Coverage for volunteer firefighters is OPTIONAL — applies only if employer has elected WC coverage under Title 39, chapter 71, AND the employer has additionally opted to include presumptive occupational disease coverage, AND the insurer agrees to provide it. At least 1 year of firefighting operations in Montana beginning on or after July 1, 2019 required. Same conditions, service minimums, and tobacco exclusion as paid firefighter rows.
Volunteer firefighter coverage is optional (employer and insurer opt-in required). Same conditions and exclusions as paid firefighter cardiovascular row.

This is a line-of-duty DEATH BENEFIT presumption only (Public Safety Employees' Death Benefits Act), not a workers' compensation presumption. Death from one of the four listed cancers is presumed to be occupationally related to firefighting and therefore a line-of-duty death, triggering a $100,000 death benefit to dependents. Cancers must be 'occupationally related to firefighting.' No rebuttability provision stated.

statute_citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. §§ 58-86A-1 through 58-86A-6 (Firefighters' Cancer Insurance Program)
notes: This is a supplemental state insurance benefits program, NOT a workers' compensation presumption. Benefits are supplemental to other health benefits. Firefighter is ineligible if simultaneously receiving WC benefits under Chapter 97 for the same cancer. Eligibility: 5 continuous years of service in an NC fire department (lapse of up to 6 months does not break continuity), OR on NC State Firefighters' Association certified roster as 'retired/nonactive' for no more than 10 years post-service. Benefits: lump sum up to $74,000 total ($37,000 per diagnosis); disability benefit of 75% of monthly salary or $5,000/month max (whichever less) for non-volunteers, or $1,500/month for volunteers, beginning 6 months after total disability, for up to 36 consecutive months. Disability benefit subordinate to other disability benefits from other sources (except private insurance purchased solely by the firefighter). Volunteer firefighters explicitly covered. Administered by the Office of the State Fire Marshal via a contracted third-party administrator.

statute_citation: N.C. Gen. Stat. §§ 58-86A-1 through 58-86A-6 (Firefighters' Cancer Insurance Program)
notes: Volunteer firefighters explicitly included in the Firefighters' Cancer Insurance Program (§ 58-86A-4(2)(b)). Disability benefit for volunteers is $1,500/month (not salary-based) for up to 36 consecutive months beginning 6 months after total disability. Lump-sum benefit structure same as non-volunteers ($37,000 per diagnosis, max $74,000 total). Same eligibility requirements apply (5 continuous years of service). Ineligible if simultaneously receiving WC benefits for the same cancer.

Tobacco users must provide yearly physician documentation of tobacco-free status for preceding 2 years to remain eligible. Periodic medical exams required by employer: every 5 years (1–10 yrs service), every 3 years (11–20 yrs), annually (21+ yrs). Medical exam results used in rebuttal.

Tobacco users must provide yearly physician documentation of tobacco-free status for preceding 2 years to remain eligible. Separate cardiac event presumption in § 65-01-15.2 applies when event occurs within 48 hours of strenuous activity or emergency response.

Cancer presumption applies to firefighters only (not law enforcement). Cancer must arise out of employment due to exposure to smoke, fumes, or carcinogenic/poisonous/toxic/chemical substances in active duty.
Bloodborne pathogen exposure must occur in the course of employment. Tobacco exclusion provision explicitly does not apply to bloodborne pathogen exposure claims.

Law enforcement officer defined as licensed peace officer employed full-time by specified agencies (BCI, game and fish, state highway patrol, parole/probation, university police, county sheriff, city PD). Cancer presumption does NOT apply to law enforcement.
Separate cardiac event presumption in § 65-01-15.2 applies when event occurs within 48 hours of strenuous law enforcement activity or emergency response; rebuttable by clear and convincing evidence.
Bloodborne pathogen exposure must occur in the course of employment. Tobacco exclusion provision explicitly does not apply to bloodborne pathogen exposure claims.

statute_citation: Neb. Rev. Stat. § 35-1001(1); Firefighter Cancer Benefits Act (LB 44)
notes: § 35-1001(1) provides prima facie evidence (rebuttable) for paid fire department members; requires exposure to IARC-identified known carcinogen while in service and carcinogen is suspected/known cause of the type of cancer. Pre-employment exam required that failed to reveal cancer. Post-separation window is 3 months (§ 35-1001(3)). Firefighter Cancer Benefits Act (FCBA) provides enhanced cash benefits for paid firefighters and volunteers deemed employees; requires 24 consecutive months service, participation in fire suppression, and use of all available PPE. FCBA post-separation eligibility: 36 months. Firefighters already provided cancer benefits under § 35-1001 are ineligible for FCBA (§ Sec. 4(3)). FCBA benefit levels: $25,000 lump sum for advanced/terminal cancer, $6,250 for carcinoma in situ/melanoma, $1,500/month disability benefit up to 36 months, $50,000 death benefit.

§ 35-1001 explicitly covers 'firefighter-paramedic' as a separate category alongside 'firefighter' — both must be members of a paid fire department. Same requirements as firefighter row: pre-employment exam, IARC carcinogen exposure, 3-month post-separation window.

Requires: (a) pre-employment/subsequent exam that failed to reveal evidence of the disease, AND (b) firefighter engaged in service within 10 years before disease onset. Post-separation window: 3 months (§ 35-1001(3)). Applies to paid fire departments only.
§ 35-1001 explicitly covers 'firefighter-paramedic' alongside 'firefighter.' Same requirements as firefighter infectious row.

Requires 5 years total service as member of a paid fire department in any city. Rebuttable presumption. Also covers police officers (separate row). 3-month post-separation window. Applies to chapter 15, article 10, §§ 16-1001 to 16-1042, and any firefighter/police pension plans under home rule charters.
§ 18-1723 covers heart, hypertension, AND respiratory defect/disease together. 5 years paid service required. 3-month post-separation window.

§ 18-1723 covers police officers of any city or village (including home rule charter cities) — no minimum years of service stated for police officers (5-year minimum stated only for firefighters). 3-month post-separation window.
§ 18-1723 covers police officers for heart, hypertension, AND respiratory defect/disease. No service minimum stated for police. 3-month post-separation window.

§ 48-101.01 is NOT a presumption — it makes mental injuries compensable for first responders but requires the employee to establish by preponderance of evidence that (a) conditions were extraordinary and unusual compared to normal employment and (b) medical causation. Covers paid and volunteer firefighters. Excluded if resulting from normal employer-employee relations (disciplinary actions, evaluations, transfers, etc.).

§ 48-101.01 covers sheriff, deputy sheriff, police officer, Nebraska State Patrol officer, volunteer and paid firefighter, and licensed EMS. Not a presumption — requires employee proof by preponderance of evidence. Excludes mental injuries from normal employer-employee relations.
§ 48-101.01 covers licensed EMS personnel (licensed under § 38-1217) providing immediate medical care. Not a presumption — requires employee proof by preponderance of evidence.

Prima facie presumption. Applies to regular, call, volunteer, and retired members of a fire department. Call/volunteer firefighters must have reasonable medical evidence on record showing freedom from disease at employment start; employer has duty to provide it, and if employer fails, firefighter still gets presumption. Retired firefighters get presumption for 5 years from retirement date if they agree to submit to requested physicals. Age 65 cutoff: benefits end one month after firefighter's 65th birthday.

Prima facie presumption for lung disease — same statute and conditions as heart disease presumption (§ 281-A:17(I)). Covers regular, call, volunteer, and retired firefighters. Same medical evidence and age 65 conditions apply.

Prima facie presumption requires 10 years of service. Tobacco-free lifestyle required (no tobacco product 4+ times/week in past 6 months, except Alaska Native/Native American religious/ceremonial use). Two tracks: (1) If dept follows NFPA 1582 exam standard: firefighter provides exam report plus tobacco-free guarantee — gets presumption. (2) If dept does NOT follow NFPA 1582: firefighter must be 10-year tobacco-free veteran AND present after-action reports documenting known carcinogen exposure — does NOT receive prima facie presumption in this case. Employer must have policy following fire standards/training commission curriculum for best practices in PPE use and cleaning. Active regular firefighters employed before Jan 1, 1997 may have NFPA 1582 exam reimbursed by Dept of Safety.

date_effective_snippet: Prima facie presumption effective January 1, 2021. 'Emergency respons
notes: Prima facie presumption effective January 1, 2021. 'Emergency response/public safety worker' under § 281-A:2, V-c includes call, volunteer, and regular firefighters. Injury definition under § 281-A:2, XI was amended to include ASD and PTSD for these workers. PTSD excluded if resulting from disciplinary action, work evaluation, job transfer, layoff, demotion, termination, or similar good-faith employer action.

date_effective_snippet: Prima facie presumption effective January 1, 2021. 'Emergency respons
notes: Prima facie presumption effective January 1, 2021. 'Emergency response/public safety worker' definition under § 281-A:2, V-c includes law enforcement officers certified under RSA 106-L.

date_effective_snippet: Prima facie presumption effective January 1, 2021. 'Emergency respons
notes: Prima facie presumption effective January 1, 2021. 'Emergency response/public safety worker' definition under § 281-A:2, V-c explicitly includes certified county corrections officers.

date_effective_snippet: Prima facie presumption effective January 1, 2021. 'Emergency respons
notes: Prima facie presumption effective January 1, 2021. 'Emergency response/public safety worker' definition under § 281-A:2, V-c includes rescue or ambulance workers: ambulance service, emergency medical personnel, first responder service, and volunteer personnel.

Rebuttable presumption. Applies to permanent or temporary members of paid or part-paid fire departments. Emergency defined broadly: any circumstances requiring immediate action to prevent loss of life, property destruction, or criminal violations — including fire suppression, firemanic drills, criminal apprehension, and medical/rescue service. Presumption is event-specific (during emergency response), not a general occupational disease presumption.
Rebuttable presumption. Explicitly covers members of volunteer fire companies. Same emergency-response-specific scope as paid firefighter row.
Rebuttable presumption. Applies to permanent or temporary members of paid or part-paid police departments, as well as special, reserve, or auxiliary police doing volunteer duty.
Rebuttable presumption. Applies to members of volunteer first aid or rescue squads.

Prima facie presumption (rebuttable by preponderance of evidence). Applies to paid, part-paid, and volunteer firefighters. 7 years of service required. Cancer type must be causable by exposure to heat, radiation, or a known/suspected IARC carcinogen. Employer may require firefighter to undergo reasonable testing at employer's expense; failure to require such testing does not adversely affect presumption. Firefighters with less than 7 years who are exposed to known carcinogens/cancer-causing radiation are covered under a separate provision (section 6 of the act, referenced but not fully quoted).
§ 34:15-31.8 explicitly covers paid, part-paid, AND volunteer fire department members. Same 7-year service requirement and conditions as paid firefighter cancer row.

Presumption of occupational disease (rebuttable by satisfactory proof). Originally applied to volunteer fire department members per statutory text (§ 34:15-43.2), but NJ Supreme Court in Lindquist v. City of Jersey City Fire Department, 175 N.J. 244 (2003), extended the presumption to paid firefighters as well. Requirements: (1) disease develops/first manifests while member is active, (2) member had pre-entry medical exam showing no such disease (or passed exam within 180 days of statute's effective date for prior members). Disease must manifest within 90 days from the event medically determined to be the cause.

Statutory text of § 34:15-43.2 explicitly applies to volunteer fire department members. Pre-entry medical exam required showing no respiratory disease (or exam within 180 days of statute effective date for prior members). Disease must manifest within 90 days from causative event.

Presumption covers 'public safety worker' which includes firefighters (paid, part-paid, volunteer). Applies to injury/illness/death from vaccines (including smallpox) administered as part of a bioterrorism-preparedness or epidemic inoculation program, or transmission from another inoculated employee/public member. Rebuttable by preponderance showing administration not linked to injury. Statute does not authorize mandatory inoculation. Included under 'other' due to vaccine/biological warfare context.

Applies to 'public safety worker' which includes paid, partially-paid, and volunteer police departments including State Police, and correctional facility officers. Same inoculation program scope as firefighter row.

Applies to 'public safety worker' which includes basic or advanced medical technicians of a first aid or rescue squad, and nurses/EMTs responding to a catastrophic incident (volunteer or employed). Same inoculation program scope.

Applies to 'public safety worker' including firefighters. Worker must demonstrate: (1) exposure to known carcinogen/cancer-causing radiation/radioactive substance due to fire, explosion, spill, or other means during employment, AND (2) injury/illness/death manifested during employment. Rebuttable by preponderance that exposure not linked to injury. Employer must maintain deployment records for sites where known carcinogens were present under hazmat right-to-know requirements.
