Applies to 'public safety worker' including police (paid, part-paid, volunteer), State Police, and corrections. Same exposure and manifestation requirements as firefighter cancer exposure row.

Applies only to full-time non-volunteer firefighters employed by state or local government who have taken the firefighter oath. Each specific cancer type has a different minimum years-of-service threshold: brain cancer 10 yrs, bladder cancer 12 yrs, kidney cancer 15 yrs, colorectal cancer 10 yrs, non-Hodgkin's lymphoma 15 yrs, leukemia 5 yrs, ureter cancer 12 yrs, testicular cancer 5 yrs (if diagnosed before age 40 with no evidence of anabolic steroid/HGH use), breast cancer 5 yrs (if diagnosed before age 40 without BRCA1/BRCA2 predisposition), esophageal cancer 10 yrs, multiple myeloma 15 yrs. Condition must not have been revealed during pre-employment or subsequent OSHA medical screening exam. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence that firefighter engaged in conduct/activities outside employment that posed significant risk of contracting the condition. Medical treatment provided as job-related unless/until court determines presumption does not apply; if court rejects presumption, employer's WC carrier reimbursed by firefighter's health plan.

Applies only to full-time non-volunteer firefighters employed by state or local government who have taken the firefighter oath. No minimum years-of-service threshold stated for infectious diseases — condition must appear and be diagnosed after entry into employment. Condition must not have been revealed during pre-employment or subsequent OSHA medical screening. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence showing firefighter's outside conduct posed significant risk.

Applies only to full-time non-volunteer firefighters employed by state or local government who have taken the firefighter oath. PTSD must be diagnosed by a physician or psychologist and must result in physical impairment, primary or secondary mental impairment, or death. No minimum years-of-service threshold. Condition must not have been revealed during pre-employment or subsequent OSHA medical screening. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

Applies only to full-time non-volunteer firefighters employed by state or local government who have taken the firefighter oath. Event-specific presumption: heart injury or stroke must occur within 24 hours of: fighting a fire, responding to an alarm, returning from an alarm call, engaging in supervised physical training, or responding to/performing in a non-fire emergency. Presumption does NOT apply if employer lacks a current physical training program AND firefighter lacks a current OSHA medical screening allowing participation in that program. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

Exposure to known or reasonably anticipated carcinogen must also be demonstrated. Subsection 2 provides a non-exclusive list of carcinogen-cancer associations. For post-retirement claims under sub. 7 (retirements on/after 7/1/2019): compensation limited to medical benefits only, no wage-loss benefits. Pre-employment exam not required per statute text for cancer (distinguished from lung/heart).

Volunteer firefighters must be entitled to WC benefits under NRS 616A.145. Post-termination formula for volunteers is always the pre-7/1/2019 formula (3 months/year, max 60 months). Exposure to known carcinogen must also be demonstrated.

Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for full-time salaried firefighters with 5+ years continuous uninterrupted service. 2-year minimum service threshold for non-conclusive compensability. Pre-employment exam required (periodic exams also required — every even year until 40, then annually). Failure to correct predisposing conditions when ordered may exclude the employee. Partial disability may elect permanent total disability benefit under NRS 616C.440.

Volunteers are not required to take the thorough lung-function test component of the physical exam (subsection 3). Conclusive presumption under subsection 5 applies only to 'full-time continuous, uninterrupted and salaried' firefighters, so volunteers have the base compensability provision only (2-year threshold, causal relationship needed).

Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for full-time salaried police officers with 5+ years continuous uninterrupted service before date of disablement. Same physical exam requirements as firefighters apply.

Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for full-time continuous, uninterrupted and salaried firefighters with 5+ years before date of disablement. Annual physical exam including heart exam required. Failure to correct predisposing conditions when ordered may exclude employee. Claims may be reopened at any time during claimant's life for changed circumstances. NRS 616C.180 (mental injury/stress) does not apply to claimants under this section (subsection 7 of 616C.180).

Volunteer firefighters covered under subsection 2 (not the conclusive presumption in subsection 1). Requires causal relationship by competent evidence that disability/death arose from performance of volunteer duties. Age limit: must not have reached age 55 before disease onset. Physical exam required once every 3 years after initial examination (subsection 4). Volunteer must be entitled to WC benefits under NRS 616A.145.

Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for full-time continuous, uninterrupted and salaried police officers with 5+ years before date of disablement. Annual physical exam including heart exam required.

Coverage under 617.481 requires demonstrated exposure during course and scope of employment, reporting to employer, and a two-step testing protocol. Employee excluded if: refuses testing, is eligible for other WC benefits, or clear and convincing evidence shows disease did not arise from employment. 'Exposure' defined as introduction of blood/infectious materials via skin, eye, mucous membrane, or parenteral contact; includes airborne TB contact.

Same two-step exposure/testing protocol as firefighters. 617.481 covers any 'person employed in this State' who contracts a contagious disease during course and scope of employment; police officers are included by the general employment definition.

Same two-step exposure/testing protocol. 617.481 covers any employee in Nevada who contracts a listed contagious disease during course and scope of employment; EMTs/paramedics employed by public safety agencies are included.

Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for salaried firefighters with 5+ continuous years before date of disability/death. Annual blood tests required (hepatitis C annually; hepatitis A and B annually unless vaccinated). Employer must provide and pay for vaccination for A and B. Persons diagnosed with hepatitis upon employment are excluded unless a different hepatitis strain is later diagnosed.

Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for police officers with 5+ continuous years before date of disability/death. Includes sheriffs, deputy sheriffs, metropolitan police officers, and city police. Same exam/vaccination requirements as firefighters.

Conclusive (irrebuttable) presumption for emergency medical attendants (licensed attendants or certified EMTs/advanced EMTs under NRS chapter 450B whose primary duties are emergency medical services) with 5+ continuous years. Same exam/vaccination requirements.

Not a pure presumption — requires clear and convincing medical/psychiatric evidence that employee has a mental injury caused by extreme stress from directly witnessing: (1) death/aftermath of violent event (homicide, suicide, mass casualty), or (2) grievous bodily harm shocking the conscience. Also covers responding to a mass casualty incident for state/political subdivision employees. Does not apply to claimants under NRS 617.457 (heart disease). Employer must provide mental health educational training to first responders including volunteers. Salaried and volunteer firefighters, police officers, emergency dispatchers, and EMTs/paramedics employed by public safety agencies are covered as 'first responders.'
Definition of 'first responder' explicitly includes volunteer firefighters. Same evidentiary standards as salaried firefighters.
Police officers are explicitly included in the 'first responder' definition. Also covers emergency dispatchers/call takers employed by law enforcement or public safety agencies.
EMTs and paramedics employed by a public safety agency in Nevada are explicitly included in the 'first responder' definition under subsection 8(b)(4).

statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-a
date_sunset_snippet: Statute nominally expired August 31, 1976 but kept alive per
notes: Statute nominally expired August 31, 1976 but kept alive per § 480. Member must have passed pre-entry physical that failed to reveal evidence of heart disease. Covers any member performing 'fire service' as defined in the retirement system (§ 302(11)(a,b,c,d,g,h)). Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence.

statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-a
date_sunset_snippet: Statute nominally expired August 31, 1976 but kept alive per
notes: Statute nominally expired August 31, 1976 but kept alive per § 480. Applies to 'recently employed' policeman who sustains disability while employed. Member must have passed pre-entry physical showing no heart disease. Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence.

statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-d
date_sunset_snippet: Statute nominally expired July 1, 2005 but may be kept ali
notes: Statute nominally expired July 1, 2005 but may be kept alive by NY RSS § 480, which extends all temporary retirement benefits expiring 1974–2011 (same mechanism as § 363-a cardiovascular). Active status requires independent legal verification — original IAFF source characterized this provision as historical and inactive. Pre-employment exam required. Covers paid firefighters only.

statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-dd
notes: Covers any police officer or firefighter covered by § 363 who contracts HIV, TB, or hepatitis after contact with members of the public where there may have been bodily fluid exposure. Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence.

statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-dd
notes: Same provision as firefighters under § 363-dd. Covers police officers under § 363 who contract HIV, TB, or hepatitis after public contact with possible bodily fluid exposure. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-f
date_sunset_snippet: Statute nominally expired July 1, 2008 but may be kept ali
notes: Statute nominally expired July 1, 2008 but may be kept alive by NY RSS § 480, which extends all temporary retirement benefits expiring 1974–2011 (same mechanism as § 363-a cardiovascular). Active status requires independent legal verification — original IAFF source characterized this provision as historical and inactive. Pre-employment or subsequent physical required showing no evidence of lung condition. Applies to uniformed members of paid fire departments.

statute_citation: NY Retirement and Social Security Law § 363-ff
notes: Covers paid firefighters. Pre-employment exam required showing no evidence of Parkinson's Disease. Disability or death covered. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

Covers paid and volunteer firefighters. Applies to exposures after January 1, 1967. Requires prior injurious exposure in Ohio employment. Requires referral to qualified medical specialist before award. Compensation only for temp total, perm total, or death.

Covers paid and volunteer firefighters. Applies to exposures after January 1, 1967. Requires prior injurious exposure in Ohio employment. Requires referral to qualified medical specialist before award. Compensation only for temp total, perm total, or death.
Covers paid and volunteer police officers (municipal, township, county). Requires prior injurious exposure in Ohio employment after January 1, 1967.
Covers paid and volunteer police officers (municipal, township, county). Requires prior injurious exposure in Ohio employment after January 1, 1967.

Rebuttable if: (1) outside-duty tobacco/high-risk exposure was probably a significant factor; (2) no IARC 1/2A carcinogen exposure shown; (3) cancer preexisted employment; (4) firefighter is age 70 or older. Applies to hazardous duty firefighters only. 'Hazardous duty' defined per 5 C.F.R. 550.902.

Presumption is for disability pension purposes (pension system statute). Applies to municipal fire department members. No presumption if member fails to submit evidence of pre-employment physical. Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence. Note: § 85A-14 (workers' comp system) requires cardiovascular events to be the major cause from extraordinary/unusual work exertion — not a presumption, effectively adverse to claimant.

Presumption is for disability pension purposes. Applies to municipal fire department members. No presumption if member fails to submit evidence of pre-employment physical. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

date_effective_snippet: Infectious disease provisions effective November 10, 1999. Defined: hepatitis
notes: Infectious disease provisions effective November 10, 1999. Defined: hepatitis, HIV, meningitis, tuberculosis. No presumption if member fails to submit evidence of pre-employment physical. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

Presumption is for disability pension purposes. Applies to municipal fire department members. No presumption if member fails to submit evidence of pre-employment physical. Rebuttable by competent evidence.

Applies to firefighters of any political division with 5+ years employment. Pre-employment (or subsequent) physical must not have revealed evidence of condition. Denial must be based on clear and convincing medical evidence that cause is unrelated to employment. Amended by 2021 Act, applies to claims filed on or after effective date.

Applies to firefighters of any political division with 5+ years employment. Pre-employment (or subsequent) physical must not have revealed evidence of condition. Denial must be based on clear and convincing medical evidence that cause is unrelated to employment. Amended by 2021 Act.

Applies only to NONVOLUNTEER firefighters (paid at or above state minimum wage). Expressly does NOT apply to volunteer firefighters. Diagnosis must be first by physician after July 1, 2009. Prostate cancer presumption does not apply if first diagnosed after age 55 (claimant may still prove without presumption). Tobacco rebuttable by clear and convincing evidence that tobacco was major contributing cause. Denial must be based on clear and convincing evidence condition was not caused or contributed to in material part by employment.

statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108; Act 115 of 2001; 34 Pa. Code § 130.1
date_effective_snippet: g that employee incurred Hep C prior to job-related exposure. Enacted December 20, 2001.
notes: Covers professional and volunteer firefighters. Employer may rebut by establishing an employment screening program per Dept. guidelines and showing via testing that employee incurred Hep C prior to job-related exposure. Enacted December 20, 2001.

statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108; Act 115 of 2001; 34 Pa. Code § 130.1
notes: Expressly covers volunteer fire fighters and volunteer ambulance corps personnel and volunteer rescue/lifesaving squad personnel. Employer may rebut via employment screening program showing pre-job exposure.

statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108; Act 115 of 2001; 34 Pa. Code § 130.1
notes: Covers emergency medical services personnel and paramedics (including volunteer ambulance corps and rescue/lifesaving squad personnel). Employer may rebut via employment screening program.

statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108; Act 115 of 2001; 34 Pa. Code § 130.1
notes: Covers Pennsylvania State Police officers and police officers requiring certification under 53 Pa.C.S. Chapter 21. Employer may rebut via employment screening program.

statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108; Act 115 of 2001; 34 Pa. Code § 130.1
notes: Covers Commonwealth and county correctional employees, and forensic security employees of the Dept. of Public Welfare, with duties including care, custody and control of inmates involving exposure to Hepatitis C. Employer may rebut via employment screening program.

statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108(r); Article III § 301(f)
notes: Requires 4+ years of continuous firefighting duties AND direct exposure to IARC Group 1 carcinogen AND pre-employment (or prior) physical showing no cancer. Volunteer fire company members covered but must document direct carcinogen exposure via Pennsylvania Fire Information Reporting System. Rebuttable by substantial competent evidence that cancer not caused by firefighting.

statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act § 108(r); Article III § 301(f)
notes: Volunteer fire company members explicitly covered. Must document direct IARC Group 1 carcinogen exposure via Pennsylvania Fire Information Reporting System reports. Otherwise same requirements as career firefighters.

statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act §§ 109, 301(g); effective October 29, 2025
notes: Effective October 29, 2025. 'First responder' includes active volunteer or employed fire company member responding to emergency calls. PTSI must result from a 'qualifying traumatic event' (serious injury/death, child harm, life threat, mass casualties, crime scene). Must be diagnosed by licensed psychologist or psychiatrist. Benefits capped at 104 weeks. PTSI from disciplinary action or employment termination not compensable. Not required to be an 'abnormal working condition.'

statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act §§ 109, 301(g); effective October 29, 2025
notes: Active volunteers of qualifying fire companies explicitly included in 'first responder' definition. Same terms as career firefighters.

statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act §§ 109, 301(g); effective October 29, 2025
notes: Active volunteer or employed EMS provider (designated as primary municipal provider or dispatched by PSAP/under mutual aid) included in 'first responder' definition. Benefits capped at 104 weeks.

statute_citation: Pennsylvania Workers' Compensation Act §§ 109, 301(g); effective October 29, 2025
notes: Covers Pennsylvania State Police officers and peace officers (18 Pa.C.S. § 501) who respond to emergency calls. Benefits capped at 104 weeks.

Covers uniformed members of PAID fire departments only. City or town may adopt this presumption by ordinance — not automatically statewide. Presumption is rebuttable by competent evidence. Pre-employment (or subsequent) physical must not have revealed evidence of condition.

Rebuttable by clear and convincing evidence. For firefighters hired AFTER July 1, 2023: presumption does not apply if (1) pre-employment physical revealed heart disease or hypertension, or (2) firefighter regularly/habitually used tobacco products during the 5 years prior to diagnosis. No such restrictions for firefighters hired before July 1, 2023.

CONCLUSIVE (irrebuttable) presumption under § 45-19.1-4 — any type of cancer in a firefighter is conclusively presumed occupational. For firefighters hired AFTER July 22, 2020: presumption does not apply if (1) pre-employment physical revealed cancer, (2) fewer than 2 years of employment, or (3) regular/habitual tobacco use in 5 years prior to diagnosis. Applies retroactively to cancer claims pending on or after July 22, 2020. Covers active firefighters (during service) and retired municipal firefighters (§ 45-19.1-3 — covers retired members retroactively).

PTSD must be diagnosed by licensed mental health professional with a master's degree. Must arise from exposure to potentially traumatic events in course of employment or off-duty emergency assistance within Rhode Island. Presumption is rebuttable by fair preponderance of evidence that PTSI/PTSD is not job-related. PTSD arising from disciplinary action, work evaluation, job transfer, layoff, demotion, termination or adverse job action not covered.

Explicitly covers police officers (including chiefs, municipal officers, deputy sheriffs, fugitive task force, capitol police, environmental police criminal investigators, airport police). Same terms as firefighters. PTSD from adverse job actions not covered.

Infectious disease defined as any condition transmissible by association with the sick or their secretions/excretions, excluding common cold; explicitly includes HIV, HBV, HCV. Disease must develop or manifest as a result of exposure during period while fire fighter or police officer is in service of department. Covers paid and volunteer fire departments. Entitles to occupational disability benefits under ch. 19 of title 45.
Statute explicitly covers both fire fighters AND police officers. Disease must manifest during period of active service. Same definition of infectious disease as firefighter row.

date_effective_snippet: 75,000 death benefit. Implementation contingent on funding. Effective July 1, 2021.
notes: This is a supplemental insurance benefit plan (not a WC presumption per se). Covers paid and unpaid (volunteer) firefighters. Requires 5 continuous years of service in a SC fire department and active service within 10 years of diagnosis. Benefits: up to $12,000/yr reimbursement for out-of-pocket expenses, $20,000 one-time initial-diagnosis benefit, $75,000 death benefit. Implementation contingent on funding. Effective July 1, 2021.

Firefighter must be a bona fide member of a municipal, county, state, port authority, or fire control district fire department. Anyone joining after May 29, 1968 must have been under age 37 at entry and passed a physical exam showing no evidence of the condition. Condition must develop while actively fighting a fire or within 24 hours of last service.

Firefighter must be a bona fide member of a municipal, county, state, port authority, or fire control district fire department. Anyone joining after May 29, 1968 must have been under age 37 at entry and passed a physical exam showing no evidence of the condition. Condition must develop while actively fighting a fire or within 24 hours of last service.

Officer must be employed by a municipal, county, state, port authority, or other law enforcement agency. Must be under age 37 at entry and have passed a physical exam with risk factor assessment for coronary artery disease. If identified as high-risk but fails to undergo additional tests at own expense, loses entitlement to presumption. Cardiac incident must occur during or within 24 hours of a law enforcement incident involving unusual/extraordinary physical exertion.

Applies to officers or members of a fire department. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumed to have been suffered in line of duty unless contrary shown by competent evidence.

Applies to officers or members of a fire department. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal any evidence of the condition. Presumed to have been suffered in line of duty unless contrary shown by competent evidence.

This section pertains solely to pension matters under Chapter 9-16 and does not restrict claimant's ability to file for benefits under chapter 62-8 or change the burden of proof established in § 62-8-12. Applies to officers or members of a fire department. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal any evidence of cancer.

Applies to full-time employees of a regular fire department in a state, municipal corporation, or political subdivision that has established compensation beyond workers' compensation. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal evidence of the condition. Contrary shown by competent medical evidence.

Applies to full-time employees of a regular fire department in a state, municipal corporation, or political subdivision that has established compensation beyond workers' compensation. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal evidence of the condition.

Applies to EMTs and paramedics employed by a county with population over 400,000 (per 1980 or subsequent federal census) or a municipal corporation within such county, within the fire department, where compensation beyond WC has been established. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal evidence of condition.

Applies to full-time paid firefighters in fire departments recognized by state fire marshal's office and manned by full-time paid employees. Must have 5+ consecutive years of service. Must have been exposed to heat, smoke, fumes, or carcinogenic/poisonous/toxic/chemical substances while performing firefighter duties. Cancer screening exams required by condition type: NHL/colon/skin/multiple myeloma exam required after July 1 2019; leukemia/testicular exam required after July 1 2022; prostate/breast/pancreatic exam required after July 1 2025. Secondary employment or lifestyle habits may be considered. Rebuttable by competent medical evidence; oncologist (board certified) for death claims.

Applies only to counties with a metropolitan form of government with population 400,000+ (per 1980 or subsequent federal census) — effectively Nashville/Davidson County. Applies to full-time firefighters required to work in toxic environments. May be established by ordinance. Pre-employment physical must have failed to reveal evidence of cancer. Does not affect rights under § 7-51-201.

Applies to full-time firefighters, paramedics, EMTs employed by state or political subdivision. Requires prior negative test for infectious disease before diagnosis. Worker must file a sworn declaration that they have not engaged in any high-risk behavior (outside-employment exposure, unsafe sexual practices, IV drug use, blood transfusion). Post-termination coverage: 1 year from last service date. Does not apply to death benefits under Tennessee consolidated retirement system or state death benefit. Pre-employment HIV testing required from July 1 2012; all infectious diseases from July 1 2015.

Applies to full-time paramedics and EMTs employed by state or political subdivision. Same terms as firefighter row under § 7-51-209. Requires prior negative test for infectious disease. Worker must file a sworn declaration excluding high-risk behaviors. Post-termination coverage: 1 year from last service date.

date_effective_snippet: es not apply to workers' compensation plans under title 50. Effective July 1, 2018.
notes: Tennessee Public Safety Behavioral Health Act requires public safety employers (including those employing firefighters and EMTs) to provide at least 10 PTSD treatment sessions via health benefits, maintain lists of qualified mental health providers, and prohibit retaliation. This is a treatment access mandate, NOT a WC presumption. Does not apply to workers' compensation plans under title 50. Effective July 1, 2018.

date_effective_snippet: hters. Treatment access mandate only, not a WC presumption. Effective July 1, 2018.
notes: Same as firefighter row — Tennessee Public Safety Behavioral Health Act applies to 'public safety employees' defined as emergency medical workers and professional firefighters. Treatment access mandate only, not a WC presumption. Effective July 1, 2018.

Cannot be rebutted by evidence that immunization was not required by employer, not required by law, or received voluntarily. 5-year service and pre-employment exam requirements apply via § 607.052 applicability section. Disease must be discovered during employment per § 607.052(a)(3). Tobacco exclusion applies under § 607.052(b)(4).

Same terms as firefighter row. EMTs and emergency medical technicians covered under § 607.052 applicability. Cannot be rebutted by evidence immunization was voluntary. 5-year service and pre-employment exam requirements per § 607.052. Volunteer firefighters covered if meeting activity thresholds (40% of drills, 25% of calls).

§ 607.054 covers TB (infectious) and other lung/respiratory diseases (respiratory) in one statutory provision; split into two rows per schema rules. Rebuttal per § 607.058 requires preponderance of evidence that non-service cause was a substantial factor; ALJ must consider whether a qualified expert relying on evidence-based medicine so stated. Tobacco exclusion under § 607.052(b)(4): does not apply if disease is known to be tobacco-caused and firefighter/EMT or their smoking spouse used tobacco during the marriage. Disease must be discovered during employment.

Same statute as TB/infectious row; this row captures the non-TB respiratory coverage. Statute language: 'any other disease or illness of the lungs or respiratory tract that has a statistically positive correlation with service as a firefighter or emergency medical technician.' Tobacco exclusion and discovery-during-employment requirement apply.
Same terms as firefighter TB row under § 607.054. Applies to EMTs covered under § 607.052.
Same terms as firefighter respiratory row under § 607.054. Tobacco exclusion and discovery-during-employment requirement apply.

Firefighter must have regularly responded on scene to fire calls, OR regularly responded to an event involving documented release of radiation or a known/suspected carcinogen. Rebuttal per § 607.058 requires preponderance of evidence; ALJ must consider whether a qualified expert relying on evidence-based medicine stated the opinion. Tobacco exclusion: disease known to be caused by tobacco use bars the presumption if firefighter/EMT or smoking spouse used tobacco.
Same terms as firefighter row under § 607.055. Tobacco exclusion applies.

Requires that while on duty, firefighter was engaged in nonroutine stressful or strenuous physical activity (fire suppression, rescue, hazmat, EMS, emergency response, or nonroutine training exercise) AND the AMI/stroke occurred during that activity. Does not include clerical, administrative, or nonmanual activities. Tobacco exclusion applies.
Same terms as firefighter row under § 607.056. AMI/stroke must occur during nonroutine strenuous activity while on duty. Tobacco exclusion applies.

Applies to firefighters subject to certification by Texas Commission on Fire Protection whose principal duties are firefighting and aircraft crash and rescue, employed by a political subdivision. PTSD must be diagnosed as caused by an event in course and scope of employment, and preponderance of evidence must show the event was a substantial contributing factor. Mental/emotional injury from legitimate personnel action (transfer, promotion, demotion, termination) is not compensable per § 408.006.

Applies to persons licensed under Chapter 773, Health and Safety Code, as emergency care attendants, EMTs, EMT-intermediates, EMT-paramedics, or licensed paramedics, employed by a political subdivision. Same PTSD diagnostic and causation requirements as firefighter row.

Applies to peace officers under Article 2.12, Code of Criminal Procedure, employed by a political subdivision. Same PTSD diagnostic and causation requirements. Mental/emotional injury from legitimate personnel action is not compensable.

Volunteer firefighters explicitly covered under § 607.052(f). Must participate in minimum 40% of drills and 25% of fire or emergency calls while on call. Same terms as paid firefighter row under § 607.053.

Volunteer firefighters explicitly covered under § 607.052(f); must participate in minimum 40% of drills and 25% of fire or emergency calls while on call. Same terms as paid firefighter TB row under § 607.054.

Volunteer firefighters explicitly covered under § 607.052(f); must participate in minimum 40% of drills and 25% of fire or emergency calls while on call. Same terms as paid firefighter respiratory row under § 607.054.

Volunteer firefighters explicitly covered under § 607.052(f). Must participate in minimum 40% of drills and 25% of fire or emergency calls while on call. Must have regularly responded to fire calls or events involving radiation/carcinogens. Same terms as paid firefighter row under § 607.055.

Volunteer firefighters explicitly covered under § 607.052(f). Must participate in minimum 40% of drills and 25% of fire or emergency calls while on call. AMI/stroke must occur during nonroutine strenuous activity while on duty. Same terms as paid firefighter row under § 607.056.

Covers paid firefighters only (2,080 hrs/year). A firefighter who received a service-connected disability for 6+ months due to violence or illness other than heart/lung/respiratory, then returned to service, cannot invoke the presumption for heart/lung/respiratory for 2 years after return unless clear and convincing evidence of direct causation from firefighter service.

Same statute and same conditions as cardiovascular row; lung disease and respiratory tract condition covered alongside heart disease. Paid firefighters only (2,080 hrs/year). 2-year re-entry exclusion applies (see cardiovascular row).

Covers paid and volunteer firefighters (34A-3-113 references 67-20-2(5)(b)(ii) which includes volunteers). Annual physical examinations required during employment. Must have regularly responded to firefighting or emergency calls within the 8-year period. Tobacco exclusion: no tobacco use for 8 years preceding report to employer. If employed by multiple employers, last-substantial-exposure employer/insurer is liable. Limited cancer list: pharynx, esophagus, lung, mesothelioma only.
Statute defines firefighter to include volunteer members per 67-20-2(5)(b)(ii). Same 8-year service and annual exam requirements apply. Tobacco exclusion: no tobacco use for 8 years preceding report.

Two pathways: (1) Employment commenced before July 1, 1988: tests positive during employment or within 3 months post-termination; (2) Employment commenced on/after July 1, 1988: must test negative at commencement AND 3 months later, then test positive during employment or within 3 months post-termination. Agency must inform providers of provisions at commencement and termination.
Covers salaried firefighters, volunteer firefighters, and Department of Emergency Management hazardous materials officers. Rebuttable by preponderance of competent evidence to the contrary.
Volunteer firefighters explicitly named. Rebuttable by preponderance of competent evidence to the contrary.
Covers salaried and volunteer firefighters. Broad law enforcement coverage under same section (see law_enforcement row). Rebuttable presumption.
Volunteer firefighters explicitly named. Rebuttable presumption.

Covers State Police Officers' Retirement System members; county/city/town police; sheriffs/deputy sheriffs; Virginia Marine Police; conservation police; Capitol Police; Virginia ABC special agents; airport authority police (Metropolitan Washington, Norfolk); Virginia Port Authority police; campus police. Rebuttable presumption.
Covers salaried/volunteer EMS personnel where the locality has adopted an authorizing resolution. Coverage is not automatic — requires local legislative action. Rebuttable presumption.

Covers salaried/volunteer firefighters and DEM hazmat officers. Colon, brain, and testicular: no presumption for diagnosis before July 1, 2020. Bladder and thyroid: no presumption for diagnosis before July 1, 2023. Rebuttable presumption.
Volunteer firefighters explicitly named. Same effective-date restrictions as salaried firefighters for colon, brain, testicular, bladder, thyroid. Rebuttable presumption.

Covers State Police Officers' Retirement System members who handle hazardous materials, infectious substances, radiological agents, fentanyl, or methamphetamine; commercial vehicle enforcement officers/motor carrier safety troopers; arson/bomb investigators (state police); full-time enforcement division motor vehicle officers. Rebuttable presumption.
Covers salaried/volunteer firefighters. Requires documented occupational exposure to blood or body fluids. Rebuttable presumption.
Volunteer firefighters explicitly named. Requires documented occupational exposure. Rebuttable presumption.
Covers salaried/volunteer EMS personnel. Requires documented occupational exposure. Rebuttable presumption.

Covers State Police Retirement System members; county/city/town police; sheriffs/deputies; Virginia Marine Police; conservation police; Capitol Police; ABC special agents; airport authority police; Virginia Port Authority police; campus police. Requires documented occupational exposure. Rebuttable presumption.
Correctional officers covered. Presumption does not apply for diagnosis before July 1, 2020. Requires documented occupational exposure. Rebuttable presumption.

Salaried/volunteer firefighters. Requires positive diagnostic test, appropriate incubation period, and symptoms requiring medical treatment. Vaccine refusal disqualifies from presumption. Rebuttable presumption.
Law enforcement officers as defined in § 9.1-101. Requires positive diagnostic test, incubation period, and symptoms requiring medical treatment. Vaccine refusal disqualifies. Rebuttable presumption.
Correctional officers and regional jail officers. Requires positive diagnostic test, incubation period, and symptoms requiring medical treatment. Vaccine refusal disqualifies. Rebuttable presumption.

Covers salaried firefighters (including special forest wardens, EMS personnel, fire scene investigators) and volunteer firefighters/EMS. Requires diagnosis by board-certified psychiatrist or licensed psychologist. Qualifying events: serious bodily injury/death, incidents involving minors, immediate life threat, mass casualties, or crime scene investigation on or after July 1, 2020. Excludes PTSD from personnel actions. Not a true rebuttable presumption — requires diagnosis + qualifying event criteria (no presumption of causation, just compensability pathway).

Volunteer EMS and volunteer firefighters explicitly covered. Same qualifying event criteria as salaried firefighters. Not a traditional rebuttable presumption — compensability pathway requiring diagnosis + qualifying event.
EMS personnel explicitly named under the firefighter definition in this section. Same qualifying event criteria apply.

Law-enforcement officer definition is broad: State Police Officers' Retirement System members; county/city/town police; sheriffs/deputy sheriffs; Dept. of Emergency Management hazmat officers; city sergeants of Richmond; Virginia Marine Police; conservation police officers; Capitol Police; VA ABC special agents; MWAA police; Norfolk Airport Authority police; Virginia Port Authority sworn officers; campus police (public institutions). Qualifying events (on/after July 1, 2020): serious bodily injury/death, incidents involving minors, immediate life threat, mass casualties, crime scene investigation. Not a traditional rebuttable presumption — compensability pathway requiring diagnosis by mental health professional + qualifying event criteria. Excludes PTSD from personnel actions.
Condition must become symptomatic within 72 hours of service in the line of duty.

Tobacco exclusion: no use within 10 years of diagnosis date. Age restriction: under 65 at diagnosis. Pre-employment exam per American Cancer Society recommendations; existing employees had until 2 years after July 1, 2007. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence that cancer was caused by non-service-connected risk factors.

Requires documented occupational exposure to aerosolized airborne agents. Tobacco exclusion: no use within 10 years of diagnosis. Vaccine refusal eliminates presumption unless medically contraindicated.

Statute covers 'rescue workers and ambulance squad members'; credential level not specified. Requires documented occupational exposure. Tobacco exclusion: no use within 10 years of diagnosis. Vaccine refusal eliminates presumption unless medically contraindicated.
Requires documented occupational exposure. Vaccine refusal eliminates presumption unless medically contraindicated. Covered in the same statutory section as lung disease.

Statute covers 'rescue workers and ambulance squad members'; credential level not specified. Requires documented occupational exposure. Vaccine refusal eliminates presumption unless medically contraindicated.
Must be diagnosed by a psychiatrist or psychologist. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence that PTSD was caused by non-service-connected risk factors.
Must be diagnosed by a psychiatrist or psychologist. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence that PTSD was caused by non-service-connected risk factors.

Statute covers 'rescue workers and ambulance workers.' Must be diagnosed by a psychiatrist or psychologist. Employer may rebut by preponderance of evidence that PTSD was caused by non-service-connected risk factors.

Covers firefighters as defined in RCW 41.26.030(17)(a),(b),(c),(h); private-sector firefighters in departments of 50+; and fire investigators. Tobacco exclusion: does not apply to regular users of tobacco products.

Covers firefighters and fire investigators. Timeline trigger: onset within 72 hours of exposure to smoke/fumes/toxic substances, or within 24 hours of strenuous physical exertion. Tobacco exclusion: regular users excluded.
Covers law enforcement officers as defined in RCW 41.26.030(19)(b),(c),(e). Same timeline trigger as firefighter heart condition. Tobacco exclusion applies.

Covers firefighters as defined in RCW 41.26.030(17)(a),(b),(c),(h); private-sector firefighters in departments of 50+; and fire investigators. Pre-employment exam must show no evidence of cancer, OR employer failed to provide exam. Prostate cancer age limit: diagnosed before age 50. Tobacco exclusion applies.
Covers firefighters and fire investigators. No tobacco exclusion for this condition.
Law enforcement officers as defined in RCW 41.26.030(19)(b),(c),(e). No tobacco exclusion.

Covers firefighters as defined in RCW 41.26.030(16)(a),(b),(c),(h); private-sector firefighters in departments of 50+. Pre-employment psychological exam required for employees hired after effective date, administered by psychiatrist or psychologist, ruling out PTSD from pre-employment exposures. Excluded: PTSD directly attributed to disciplinary action, work evaluation, job transfer, layoff, demotion, termination, or similar action.
Law enforcement officers as defined in RCW 41.26.030(18)(b),(c),(e). Same pre-employment psychological exam requirement and same exclusion for personnel-action-related PTSD.

Covers state, county, and municipal firefighters. Pre-employment exam must show no evidence of heart or respiratory impairment or disease. Presumption applies in proceedings for disability or death benefits under s. 40.65(2) or applicable pension/retirement systems. 'Municipal fire fighter' includes persons under s. 61.66 where firefighting duties took up at least two-thirds of working hours during the 5-year qualifying period.

Same statute and pre-employment exam requirement as cardiovascular row. Heart and respiratory impairment/disease are covered together under § 891.45(2). Covers state, county, and municipal firefighters.

Pre-employment exam must show no evidence of an infectious disease. 'Fire fighter' for this section means a state, county, or municipal firefighter covered under § 891.45 and persons under s. 61.66 whose duties as a firefighter took up at least two-thirds of working hours. Presumption applies in proceedings for disability or death benefits.

'Emergency medical service provider' means a person employed by the state or by a county or municipality who is an emergency medical technician (§ 256.01(5)) or a first responder (§ 256.01(9)). Pre-employment exam must show no evidence of an infectious disease. Presumption applies in proceedings for disability or death benefits.

Statute explicitly names 'law enforcement officer' as a covered class alongside correctional officers, EMS providers, and fire fighters. Pre-employment exam must show no evidence of infectious disease.

Statute explicitly names 'correctional officer' as a covered class. Pre-employment exam must show no evidence of infectious disease. Presumption applies in proceedings for disability or death benefits under s. 40.65(2) or applicable pension/retirement systems.

Covers state, county, and municipal firefighters. Pre-employment exam must show no evidence of cancer. System-based cancer list (not specific cancer types): skin, breasts, CNS, lymphatic, digestive, hematological, urinary, skeletal, oral, reproductive systems. Tobacco exclusion: presumption for smoking/tobacco-caused cancers does not apply to any municipal fire fighter who smokes cigarettes or uses tobacco products after January 1, 2001. 'Municipal fire fighter' under this section includes persons under s. 61.66 whose duties as a firefighter during the 10-year qualifying period took up at least two-thirds of working hours.

Covers professional firefighters only. Cardiovascular/pulmonary presumption applies if disease/injury onset occurred within 6 months of firefighting or training/drill exercise involving firefighting. Statute also notes notice is presumed sufficient when conditions are met.
Covers professional firefighters only. Pulmonary disease is enumerated alongside cardiovascular in §23-4-1(h) under the same 2-year service / 6-month firefighting-activity trigger.
Covers professional firefighters only. Requires 5 years active employment in WV, age under 65, and no tobacco use for at least 10 years. No sunset on these cancers. Rebuttable presumption.

date_sunset_snippet: SUNSET: Added by 2024 legislative session; expires July 1, 2027, unless extended by
notes: SUNSET: Added by 2024 legislative session; expires July 1, 2027, unless extended by the Legislature. Covers professional firefighters only. Requires 5 years active employment in WV, age under 65, and no tobacco use more than 6 times per calendar year for at least 10 years. Rebuttable presumption.

Coverage is employer-elective: employer must purchase PTSD coverage from its WC carrier or self-insurance program. Diagnosis must be made by a licensed psychiatrist, certified mental health nurse practitioner, or certified psychiatric physician assistant. Applies only to diagnoses on or after July 1, 2021 (or start of next policy term for which coverage was purchased). Disciplinary actions and personnel decisions excluded. Volunteer firefighters included via definition of 'employer' covering paid or volunteer employment.

Law enforcement officers are explicitly included in the definition of 'first responder' under §23-4-1f. Coverage is employer-elective. Same conditions as firefighter row: employer must purchase PTSD coverage, diagnosis by qualifying provider required, applies to diagnoses on or after July 1, 2021.

EMTs and paramedics are explicitly included in the definition of 'first responder' under §23-4-1f. Coverage is employer-elective. Same conditions as firefighter row: employer must purchase PTSD coverage, diagnosis by qualifying provider required, applies to diagnoses on or after July 1, 2021.

Emergency dispatchers are explicitly included in the definition of 'first responder' under §23-4-1f (mapped to corrections as closest category — dispatchers are not corrections officers but no better schema value exists). Coverage is employer-elective. Diagnosis by qualifying provider required, applies to diagnoses on or after July 1, 2021.

Effective July 1, 2017. Rebuttable by clear and convincing evidence that a risk factor, accident, hazard, or other cause not associated with firefighter service was the dominant cause. Tobacco exclusion: presumption does not apply if listed disease is known to be caused by smoking and firefighter was a regular tobacco user for 10+ years, or was a regular user for 10+ years and has quit fewer than 10 years ago. Listed disease category also includes case-by-case demonstration before the Wyoming workers' compensation medical commission (§27-15-102(h)).

Volunteer firefighters are explicitly included in the statutory definition of 'firefighter' under §27-15-101(a)(i). Volunteers satisfy the 10-year minimum period if they participated in at least 40% of drills and 25% of emergency calls during their service. Volunteer departments must maintain individual participation records. Same rebuttable standard and tobacco exclusion as paid firefighters.

Cardiovascular disease, acute myocardial infarction, and stroke are all listed as enumerated 'listed diseases' under §27-15-101(a)(ii)(B) and (C). Same service, pre-employment exam, post-termination, and tobacco-exclusion conditions as cancer row. Tobacco exclusion applies only if the specific listed disease is known to be caused by smoking.

Volunteer firefighters explicitly included in 'firefighter' definition under §27-15-101(a)(i). Volunteer minimum period: 40% of drills and 25% of emergency calls over 10 years. Cardiovascular disease, MI, and stroke are enumerated listed diseases. Same rebuttable standard and tobacco exclusion as paid firefighters.

Not a true presumption — this is a statutory exception to Wyoming's general exclusion of mental-mental claims. First responders (firefighters, peace officers, search and rescue, ambulance personnel) may have mental injuries recognized as compensable WC injuries if established by clear and convincing evidence with qualifying provider diagnosis. Disciplinary action, job transfers, layoffs, terminations, and similar personnel actions excluded. Benefits capped at 36 months post-diagnosis. 'First responder' defined at §27-14-102(a)(xxxi) to include firefighters (paid and volunteer).

Peace officers are explicitly included in the 'first responder' definition at §27-14-102(a)(xxxi). Same compensability standard as firefighter row — exception to mental-mental exclusion, not a presumption. Requires clear and convincing evidence and qualifying provider diagnosis. Personnel actions excluded.

Ambulance personnel (EMTs/paramedics) and search and rescue personnel are explicitly included in the 'first responder' definition at §27-14-102(a)(xxxi). Same compensability standard as firefighter row — exception to mental-mental exclusion, not a presumption. Requires clear and convincing evidence and qualifying provider diagnosis. Personnel actions excluded.
